https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/lote/listar

This method allows the user to retrieve the list of jobs requested by the user, with detailed data about each of the processes.

Operation

From the provided access token, all currently running batch processing jobs are retrieved, with detailed information (the same as that which can be seen in the GetJobDetails method) about them. The returns are paged according to the page size defined in the call.

Optional Parameter - ShowHistoricalJobs

If you also want to retrieve information about historical jobs, simply include the ShowHistoricalJobs field with the value true in the request. See the body request of the "ListJobs + HistoricalJobs" example below.

Description of main object return fields
Field	Description
TotalNumberOfCurrentJobs	Total number of jobs that are currently running
TotalNumberOfJobs	Total number of jobs already submitted by the user
Jobs	List of objects with detailed information about user jobs
Description of processing detail object return fields
Field	Description
JobId	Unique job identifier
JobName	Job name
JobUserName	User associated with the token
JobDomain	Domain associated with the token
CurrentJobStatus	Current stage (status) of the job
StatusPercentageComplete	Current job stage completeness percentage
TotalRecords	Total number of records sent in the query input
TotalQueriedRecords	Total number of records consulted
TotalParsedRecords	Total number of parsed records
TotalQueryErrors	Total number of query errors occurred
TotalParsingErrors	Total number of parsing errors occurred
FirstQueryItem	String representation of the JSON object referring to the first query performed in the job
QueryAPI	Which API was used to run this job
LastUpdateDate	Last job status update date
Possible Status of the Process

The following table describes the possible values of the CurrentJobStatus field, and the meaning of each of these values.

Value	Description
pending	The job was saved in the database, but no processing was performed on top of it
loading	The job is being loaded for processing in the query queues
loaded	The job was correctly loaded for processing in the query queues, and the user can now command the start of its execution
querying	The job is being queried
parallel_limit_reached	The job is being queried for the maximum number of parallel processes currently available. It is equivalent to querying value
paused	Execution of job queries has been paused until user commands resume
wanted	The job's queries have ended, and it is waiting for the parsing process to generate the output files
parsing	Job query results are being parsed to generate output files
done	The job execution has ended and the final result file sent
cancelled	The job execution was canceled by the user
Status Codes

The following table describes possible return codes and messages.

Code	Message	Description
0	OK	Successful execution
-1	INVALID AUTHENTICATION PARAMETERS	The access token provided in the entry is invalid
-2	BAD REQUEST FORMAT	The sent request is not well formatted
-1200	UNEXPECTED ERROR. PLEASE CONTACT OUR SUPPORT TEAM	An unexpected error has occurred. Get in touch with our service team
BODY raw
{
	"PageSize": 100,
	"PageNumber": 2
}

RECENT REQUESTS

https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/lote/salvarDefinicao

This method allows you to insert a file with a batch (batch) of records to be queried in the Data Platform processing queue. In return, the user receives a JobId, the unique identifier of this newly defined batch processing.

Operation

This method receives as input the parameters required to query a batch of records: the file with the records to be processed, the API to be queried, the format of the output file, and so on. These elements (described in more detail in the section below) are used to insert the job into the processing queue and, subsequently, to execute the queries.

The input file, which contains the records to be processed, can be submitted in multiple different formats (described in more detail in the section below).

For files exchanged via HTTP or HTTPS, the input file must be available via a public link that allows the file to be downloaded by the executing process; otherwise, the batch execution will fail.

For files made available through S3 Buckets, the access credentials provided in the API call must have permission to get and put objects in the bucket, in addition to identifying the bucket's region. In other words, for this access, we need permission for the methods: GetBucketLocation, GetObject, and PutObject .

It is possible to use all the query mechanisms available in the Data Platform's conventional API, whether for querying people, companies or validations, combined with the elements of the input files through the definition of a query template . This includes executing queries using alternative keys, sending reference dates for backtest execution and much more.

In addition to the mechanisms available in the API, Batch processing also supports query retry . The purpose of this mechanism is to support processing of very large files or files that depend on unstable information sources, allowing queries with errors to be re-executed without the need to resubmit the input file, simplifying the work of customers. It is important to remember that you are never charged for queries with errors, so retrying does not imply additional costs for customers. Queries are retried in two situations:

There was some connection failure or response timeout in the Query API call; or ;
All datasets queried returned an error code less than or equal to -1200 (see the error code table for details).

The optional parameters used for query retry are described in the table below.

The output file is made available through a link in the BigData Corp's file transfer area, which is located at https://files.bigdatacorp.com.br/go, and is available for download for up to 30 days after execution. of the job. Alternatively, if using the S3 Buckets file exchange method, the output file is deposited in the repository defined by the customer at the input.

ATTENTION: After calling this method, the file will be validated and loaded for processing, but the query process will only start when the StartJobExecution method, described below, is called.

Input Parameters

The following table describes the input parameters of this method, indicating whether they are mandatory or not, and the possible values for fields with predefined values.

Field	Mandatory?	Possible Values	Description
InputFileUrl	Yes	Input file URL	A valid URL accessible by the process. See below how to format this parameter for different transfer mechanisms.
OutputFileUrl	Not.	Output file URL	See below how to format this parameter for different transfer mechanisms.
OutputFilePassword	Not.	Any string	The password that will be required to access the output files.
InputFileDelimiter	Not. The TAB character ("\t") is the default	Any string except end-of-line delimiters ( and )	The delimiter of the input file's columns
InputFileHasHeader	Not. false is the default	true or false	Indicates whether the input file has a header
OutputFileFormat	Not. text is the default	text, json, csv or tsv	Indicates the format of the processing output file
OutputFileDelimiter	No. For text and tsv files the default delimiter is the TAB character ("\t"), for csv files the default delimiter is the comma (", ")	Any string except end-of-line delimiters ( and )	Column delimiter used in the output file. Ignored when output format is json
OutputRemoveEmptyColumns	Not. false is the default	true or false	Indicates whether columns without populated values should be removed from the output.
InputKeysHeader	Yes	Any string	Text that will be used in the header of the output file for the input values that are copied
InputFileSampleRate	Not. -1 is the default	-1 or number from 0.01 to 1	For values other than -1, it indicates the percentage of the input file that should be selected as a sample for execution
API Name	Yes	People, Companies or Validations	The API that should be called by the process
Datasets	No	List of datasets, comma separated	The names of the datasets to be queried, just as they are filled in in a query. If the datasets are not filled in, the View field must be filled in (below)
View	No	Any view already defined in the system	The View that should be used for the query. It must be filled in only in case the Datasets field (above) is empty.
QueryTemplate	Yes	A template indicating how query parameters should be sent	Each column in the input file is represented by, where x is the number (starting at zero) of the desired column. For a file with only one column, with the documents, it would be doc{{0}}
MaxQueryRetries	No	Any integer greater than 0	Used only when configuring query retry. Defines the number of times that the query for a specific CPF or CNPJ must be retried before the return is treated as an error.
QueryRetryBackoff	No	Any integer greater than 0	Used only when configuring query retry. Defines the time, in seconds, that the Batch process will wait before trying to query again the CPF or CNPJ that returned with an error. It is extremely useful for queries that have availability issues or processing delays, such as on-demand queries involving government websites.
CopyEmails	Not. Empty list is the default	An email list	The emails that should receive copies of the process execution messages, in addition to the user's email
CopyPhones	Not. Empty list is the default	A phone list	The phones that must receive the warnings of the process execution steps by SMS, in addition to the user's email. Telephones must be informed in the format (eg: 5511999999999), and only cell phones are accepted.
CopyHooks	Not. Empty list is the default	A list of json objects with details	JSON objects must contain the URL where the process execution notification will be posted, and, if applicable, a "secret" that should be posted along with the notification. Example: [ { Url: "https://myurl.com", PostbackSecret: "my-postback-secret" } ]. As many notification URLs as the user wishes can be included.
InputFileExpectedNumberOfRecords	No	An integer greater than zero	When this parameter is informed, the number of records loaded from the input file is checked and, if it is different from the number informed, processing is disabled and an error is reported to the user.
AutomaticallyStartQuerying	Not. false is the default	true or false	Flag that indicates to the system that the processing must be started as soon as the loading of the records is finished, dispensing with the call of StartJobExecution. We recommend its use only by customers with experience in performing this type of process.
APIResultLimit	Not. 1 is the default	Any integer greater than 0	This parameter is used for processing where queries are being performed using alternative keys, to allow control of the amount of results returned by key
Formatting the URLs of process input and output files

The syntax below applies to fields InputFileURL and OutputFileUrl, and is used to specify the file transfer mechanism, in addition to the parameters necessary for the transfer to be carried out successfully. We list the supported transfer mechanisms below, and the syntax for each one:

Transfer via HTTP or HTTPS: In this type of transfer, the input file will be downloaded from the link provided, and the output file will be generated on a BigDataCorp clipboard for download by the client. The following example illustrates how to fill in the required parameters:

{
  "InputFileUrl": "web{https://www.example.com/mysamplefile.txt}",
  "InputKeysHeader" : "Documento",
  "APIName": "people",
  "Datasets": "basic_data",
  "QueryTemplate": "doc{{0}}"
}


Transfer via S3 (AWS Data Storage Service): In this type of transfer, the customer can specify a return address independent of the incoming address. If the output file's URL parameter is not populated, the results file will be placed in the same bucket as the input file. The following example illustrates how to fill in the required parameters:

{
  "InputFileUrl": "file{s3://aws_access_key:aws_secret_key@bucket_name}, remotefilepath{path_to_file_in_s3}",
  "OutputFileUrl": "file{s3://aws_access_key:aws_secret_key@bucket_name}, remotefilepath{path_for_file_to_be_saved_at}",
  "InputKeysHeader" : "Documento",
  "InputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "InputFileHasHeader": false,
  "OutputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "APIName": "people",
  "Datasets": "basic_data",
  "QueryTemplate": "doc{{0}}"
}


FTP transfer: In this transfer model, the input and output address can be completely different. Parameters in parentheses "()" are optional, and if not filled in, they will assume default values. The following example illustrates how to fill in the required parameters:

{
  "InputFileUrl": "file{ftp://user(:password)@dns(:port)}, remotefilepath{path_to_file_in_ftp}",
  "OutputFileUrl": "file{ftp://user(:password)@dns(:port)}, remotefilepath{path_for_file_to_be_saved_at}",
  "InputKeysHeader" : "Documento",
  "InputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "InputFileHasHeader": false,
  "OutputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "APIName": "people",
  "Datasets": "basic_data",
  "QueryTemplate": "doc{{0}}"
}


Transfer via SFTP: In this transfer model, the input and output address can be completely different. Parameters in parentheses "()" are optional, and if not filled in, they will assume default values. The following example illustrates how to fill in the required parameters:

{
  "InputFileUrl": "file{sftp://user(:password)@dns(:port)}, remotefilepath{path_to_file_in_sftp}",
  "OutputFileUrl": "file{sftp://user(:password)@dns(:port)}, remotefilepath{path_for_file_to_be_saved_at}",
  "InputKeysHeader" : "Documento",
  "InputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "InputFileHasHeader": false,
  "OutputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "APIName": "people",
  "Datasets": "basic_data",
  "QueryTemplate": "doc{{0}}"
}


IMPORTANT! The fields of InputFileUrl and OutputFileUrl are independent. An input file present on S3 and an output file posted to an SFTP folder can be specified, or any other combination of the mechanisms listed above.

A password will be added to a return file depending on the type of destination environment. For internal environments such as S3, FTP, or SFTP, where the file is stored within the corporate environment, a password is not added to the file. However, for external environments, publicly accessible via URL, an automatically generated password is always included to ensure the security of the file.

If preferred, it is possible to specify the password to be added to a file via the 'OutputFilePassword' field when making a request, thus allowing files sent to external environments to have a manually defined password. It is worth noting that, if the 'OutputFilePassword' field is included in the request, but is empty, filled only with spaces or null, the password assigned to the file will be generated automatically.

In all cases where a password is applied to the file, it will be sent by email to the user's registered address and to the emails listed in the CopyEmails field, also present in the current request.

Input files
Formats .txt,.csv and .tsv.
Each line of the file must contain one record.
If the record has multiple columns, they must be separated by a delimiter of your choice (e.g. comma, semicolon, tab, etc.). Make sure the same delimiter is specified in the field InputKeysHeader and in the field InputFileDelimiter..
Format .xlsx (Excel spreadsheet).
When specifying the file path in the field InputFileUrl, if necessary, include the index of the desired spreadsheet right after the file extension, in the format [x], where x represents the worksheet index, starting at 1.
If no index is provided, the default value will be used. 1.
Example:
{"InputFileUrl":"web{https://www.example.com/mysamplefile.xlsx[2]}"}
Google Docs files.
Make sure the document is shared as a public file or has appropriate permissions for access via link.
Insert the direct link of the file in the field InputFileUrl. The system will download and process the content automatically.
Example:
{"InputFileUrl":"web{https://docs.google.com/document/d/example-file/edit?usp=sharing}"}
Return

The method returns an error code and message, in case a problem has occurred. Otherwise, the job's unique identifier is returned, which can be used to retrieve other information about the job or to cancel its execution (see the other Batch Processing API methods).

The following table describes the fields of the return object of this method.

Field	Description
JobId	Identifier of the created job
StatusCode	Call result code
StatusMessage	Descriptive call result message
TotalExecutionTime	Total execution time of the request on the server
RequestTimestamp	Date and time of completion of the request execution on the server
Status Codes

The following table describes possible return codes and messages.

Code	Message	Description
0	OK	Successful execution
-1	INVALID AUTHENTICATION PARAMETERS	The access token provided in the entry is invalid
-2	BAD REQUEST FORMAT	The sent request is not well formatted
-101	INVALID INPUT FILE URL	The link to the specified input file is not working
-102	INVALID OUTPUT FILE FORMAT	The specified output format is outside the allowed values
-103	INVALID SAMPLE RATE	The defined sample rate is outside the allowed values
-105	INVALID API NAME	The name of the API that will be queried by the process is outside the allowed values
-106	INVALID DATASETS	The selected dataset set is invalid
-107	INVALID QUERY TEMPLATE	The defined query element template is invalid
-108	INVALID JOB ID	The job identifier passed in the input is invalid
-109	INVALID JOB STATUS FOR OPERATION	The selected job cannot be paused or canceled due to its current status
-1200	UNEXPECTED ERROR. PLEASE CONTACT OUR SUPPORT TEAM	An unexpected error has occurred. Get in touch with our service team
Examples

Example of a request to run the Basic People Dataset query for an input file with a single column with CPFs:

{
  "InputFileUrl": "web{https://www.example.com/mysamplefile.txt}",
  "InputKeysHeader" : "Documento",
  "InputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "InputFileHasHeader": false,
  "OutputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "APIName": "people",
  "Datasets": "basic_data",
  "QueryTemplate": "doc{{0}}"
}


Example of request to query in the backtest model the basic data and emails of companies for an input file with three columns delimited by TAB: an external id, the document, and a reference date:

{
  "InputFileUrl": "web{https://www.example.com/mysamplefile.txt}",
  "InputFileDelimiter": "\t"
  "InputKeysHeader" : "Id_Externo\tDocumento\tData_Referencia",
  "InputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "InputFileHasHeader": false,
  "OutputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "APIName": "companies",
  "Datasets": "basic_data,emails",
  "QueryTemplate": "external_id{{0}},doc{{1}},referencedate{{2}}"
}


Example of request to query, using alternate keys, datasets of basic data and telephone numbers of people, for an input file with two input columns - name and date of birth (in day/month/year format) - delimited by dot- semicolon (";"):

{
  "InputFileUrl": "web{https://www.example.com/mysamplefile.txt}",
  "InputFileDelimiter": ";"
  "InputKeysHeader" : "Nome\tData_Nascimento",
  "InputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "InputFileHasHeader": false,
  "OutputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "APIName": "people",
  "Datasets": "basic_data,phones",
  "QueryTemplate": "name{{0}},birthdate{{1}},dateformat{dd/MM/yyyy}"
}


Example of a request to consult the on-demand status dataset at the Federal Revenue Service of CNPJs and return the results in JSON format, to a file with a single input column with the documents, copying any email about the execution status:

{
  "InputFileUrl": "web{https://www.example.com/mysamplefile.txt}",
  "OutputFileFormat": "json",
  "InputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "InputFileHasHeader": false,
  "OutputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "APIName": "companies",
  "Datasets": "ondemand_rf_status",
  "QueryTemplate": "doc{{0}}"
  "CopyEmails": ["email_qualquer@email.com"],
}


Example of a request to run the Basic People Dataset query for an input file made available in an S3 bucket:

{
  "InputFileUrl": "file{s3://Sample_Access_Key:Sample_Secret_Key@Sample_Bucket}, remotefilepath{Sample_Folder/mysamplefile.txt}",
  "InputKeysHeader" : "Documento",
  "InputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "InputFileHasHeader": false,
  "OutputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "APIName": "people",
  "Datasets": "basic_data",
  "QueryTemplate": "doc{{0}}"
}


Example of request to consult a predefined view in the Batch process, instead of datasets, for a file with a single input column with documents:

{
   "InputFileUrl": "file{s3://Sample_Access_Key:Sample_Secret_Key@Sample_Bucket}, remotefilepath{Sample_Folder/mysamplefile.txt}",
  "InputKeysHeader" : "Documento",
  "InputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "InputFileHasHeader": false,
  "OutputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "APIName": "people",
  "View": "minha_view_predefinida",
  "QueryTemplate": "doc{{0}}"
}


Example of request with configuration of 2 retries for each query performed, with 30 seconds of waiting between each query, for a file with a single input column with the documents:

{
  "InputFileUrl": "file{s3://Sample_Access_Key:Sample_Secret_Key@Sample_Bucket}, remotefilepath{Sample_Folder/mysamplefile.txt}",
  "InputKeysHeader" : "Documento",
  "InputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "InputFileHasHeader": false,
  "OutputFileDelimiter": "	",
  "APIName": "people",
  "Datasets": "basic_data",
  "QueryTemplate": "doc{{0}}",
  "MaxQueryRetries": 2,
  "QueryRetryBackoff": 30
}

BODY raw
{
  "InputFileUrl": "[URL do seu arquivo de entrada]",
  "InputFileDelimiter": "\t",
  "InputFileHasHeader": false,
  "OutputFileFormat": "txt",
  "OutputFileDelimiter": "\t",
  "OutputRemoveEmptyColumns": false,
  "InputKeysHeader" : "Documento",
  "InputFileSampleRate": -1,
  "CopyEmails": ["email1@teste.com.br","email2@outroteste.com.br"],
  "APIName": "companies",
  "Datasets": "basic_data,addresses_extended,phones_extended,emails_extended,activity_indicators,relationships,related_people_phones,related_people_emails",
  "QueryTemplate": "doc{{0}}"
}

Usage Statistics

Request
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/uso/

The Usage Statistics API returns information about service usage (and the limits configured for this usage) by the user performing the query, within the period specified by the user. Queries are counted dynamically, so a request made now and another made five minutes from now could potentially bring different results. Verification in very long periods (date ranges ) can impact the response time of the request.

This API returns information about the total number of requests made against the service, the number of requests that returned successfully and in error, the average response time for the requests, and an estimate of the amount that will be charged to the customer for the requests made ( this value may change due to the volume of queries performed ). In addition to the general numbers, detailed statistics are also sent for each Dataset available to the customer, allowing a detailed analysis of the service's behavior.

Finally, the API return also contains the monthly query limit configured for the user's account, and allows a follow-up of whether the query volume has already reached this limit or not.

Operation

If a parameter is not passed in the Body, all calls made by it in the current month will be counted. Alternatively, calls to the Usage Statistics API can be assigned a start date (InitialReferenceDate) and an end date (FinalReferenceDate), which will be used to compose the period in which the requests made will be counted. In addition, it can also receive a date format parameter (DateFormat), which indicates in which format the dates given in the input were filled in.

When we are composing the period, the 0:00 hours of each informed date are considered, that is, a request that receives January 1st as the starting date and December 31st as the end date will only count the requests made at 0:00 pm on the day January 1st until 12:00 am on December 31st, and calls made during the 31st will be ignored. To account for an entire year, the 1st of January of the year you wish to consult must be passed as the start date, and the 1st of January of the following year as the end date.

Examples

How to retrieve usage statistics for the current month:

POST https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/uso
Body:
{}


How to retrieve usage statistics for the month of July 2019:

POST https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/uso
Body:
{
  "InitialReferenceDate": "2019-07-01",
  "FinalReferenceDate": "2019-08-01",
  "DateFormat": "yyyy-MM-dd"
}


How to retrieve usage statistics for the year 2018:

POST https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/uso
Body:
{
  "InitialReferenceDate": "2018-01-01",
  "FinalReferenceDate": "2019-01-01",
  "DateFormat": "yyyy-MM-dd"
}

Configure User
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/monitoramento/definirUsuario
Description

This feature allows you to configure important information for the proper functioning of the Monitoring API, according to the user's needs and preferences. To update any of the authentication settings, simply add the corresponding parameter to the request and enter its new value. To avoid overwriting an already configured field, the field should not be informed in the request.

Input Parameters

The following table describes the input parameters of this method, indicating whether they are required or not.

Field	Mandatory?	Possible Values	Description
AccessToken	Yes	Authentication token in the Data Platform APIs. It can also be informed in the request header.	JWT Token
SftpPrivateKey	No	Private authentication key for SFTP servers.	Access key in OpenSSH format
Authorization Key	No	authorization key	Access key
AuthorizationToken	No	authorization token	Token
OAuthBasicToken	No	Basic OAuth Token	OAuth Token
OAuthPublicKey	No	This field must be used together with the OAuthPrivateKey, if the OAuthBasicToken is not provided	Access key
OAuthPrivateKey	No	OAuth Private Key	Access key
OAuthURLTarget	No	Authentication server access URL	url
OAuthContentType	No	Type of content that should be returned	content type
OAuthGrantType	No	Determines the type of authorization	type of permission
OAuthExpectedTokenFieldName	No	This field is used by the client to point to the field where we will retrieve the access token coming from OAuth (default value is "access_token")	Field name

The AuthorizationToken and OAuthBasicToken fields are not stored in our databases. We use the AWS key persistence service, offering greater security to user credentials.

Return

The following table describes the fields of the return object of this method.

Field	Description
User	Username
ResultStatus	Query status message
Examples

Below are some examples of using this endpoint.

POST: https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/monitoramento/definirUsuario

Request:

{
    "AccessToken": "<YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN>",
    "AuthorizationKey": "<YOUR_AUTH_KEY>",
    "AuthorizationToken": "<YOUR_AUTH_TOKEN>"
}

Response:

{
  "SubscriptionConfiguration": {
    "User": "JOHN@DOE.COM",
    "ResultStatus": "OK"
  },
  "QueryId": "1cfecda3-7cdf-4c9e-10e7-e66c5b2b74a5",
  "ElapsedMilliseconds": 0,
  "QueryDate": "2024-09-19T13:51:39.898Z",
  "Status": {
    "subscription": [
      {
        "Code": 0,
        "Message": "OK"
      }
    ]
  }
}
Request:

{
    "AccessToken": "<YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN>",
    "SftpPrivateKey": "-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
<HIDDEN>
-----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
"
}

Response:

{
  "SubscriptionConfiguration": {
    "User": "JOHN@DOE.COM",
    "ResultStatus": "OK"
  },
  "QueryId": "1cfecda3-7cdf-4c9e-10e7-e66c5b2b74a5",
  "ElapsedMilliseconds": 0,
  "QueryDate": "2024-09-19T13:51:39.898Z",
  "Status": {
    "subscription": [
      {
        "Code": 0,
        "Message": "OK"
      }
    ]
  }
}

POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/monitoramento/listar
Description

This feature allows you to list all active monitoring logs for a domain. It returns information about parameters, datasets, APIs, and more. Logs are displayed in order of creation, from oldest to most recent. Filters and paging can be used to analyze larger volumes of logs.

Input Parameters

The following table describes the input parameters of this method, indicating whether they are required or not.

Field	Mandatory?	Description	Possible Values
AccessToken	Yes	Authentication token in the Data Platform APIs. It can also be informed in the request header.	JWT Token
PageSize	No	The maximum number of records that will be returned per request (default 50 records)	Integer greater than 0.
Cursor	No	This field receives a monitoring identification ( SubscriptionId ), which will be used as a reference to obtain other records generated from the creation date of this reference.	SubscriptionId
FilterByParameters	No	The search filter using a specific parameter in the associated records	Same value as parameter "q" of queries on datasets
Return

The following table describes the fields of the return object of this method.

Field	Description
ActiveSubscriptions	List of objects with detailed information for each monitoring
ActiveSubscriptions[n].SubscriptionId	Unique monitoring id
ActiveSubscriptions[n].OriginalQueryId	QueryId of the call that originated this monitoring
ActiveSubscriptions[n].Api	API used in this monitoring (people, companies)
ActiveSubscriptions[n].Locale	Monitoring region (pt-br, mx-es)
ActiveSubscriptions[n].Parameters	Parameters sent in the call that originated this monitoring
ActiveSubscriptions[n].Datasets	Datasets enrolled in monitoring
ActiveSubscriptions[n].TargetHook	Return hook configuration used
Examples

Below are some examples of using this endpoint.

POST: https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/monitoramento/listar

Request:

{
    // Empty
}

Response:

{
  "ActiveSubscriptions": [
    {
      "SubscriptionId": "66eb250c116bae298ca4dafe",
      "OriginalQueryId": "b9886b7d-c796-43ac-b97d-c5006a33ecf5",
      "Api": "people",
      "Locale": "pt-br",
      "Parameters": "doc{CPF}",
      "Datasets": "basic_data.listen(TaxIdStatus)",
      "TargetHook": "email{john@doe.com}"
    }
  ],
  "QueryId": "1cfecda3-7cdf-4c9e-10e7-e66c5b2b74a5",
  "ElapsedMilliseconds": 0,
  "QueryDate": "2024-09-19T13:51:39.898Z",
  "Status": {
    "subscription": [
      {
        "Code": 0,
        "Message": "OK"
      }
    ]
  }
}
Request:

{
    "FilterByParameters": "doc{CPF}"
}

Response:

{
  "ActiveSubscriptions": [
    {
      "SubscriptionId": "66eb250c116bae298ca4dafe",
      "OriginalQueryId": "b9886b7d-c796-43ac-b97d-c5006a33ecf5",
      "Api": "people",
      "Locale": "pt-br",
      "Parameters": "doc{CPF}",
      "Datasets": "basic_data.listen(TaxIdStatus)",
      "TargetHook": "email{john@doe.com}"
    }
  ],
  "QueryId": "1cfecda3-7cdf-4c9e-10e7-e66c5b2b74a5",
  "ElapsedMilliseconds": 0,
  "QueryDate": "2024-09-19T13:51:39.898Z",
  "Status": {
    "subscription": [
      {
        "Code": 0,
        "Message": "OK"
      }
    ]
  }
}
Request:

{
    "Cursor": "66c33ea6793e9579617c8165",
    "PageSize": 10
}

Response:

{
  "ActiveSubscriptions": [
    {
      "SubscriptionId": "66eb250c116bae298ca4dafe",
      "OriginalQueryId": "b9886b7d-c796-43ac-b97d-c5006a33ecf5",
      "Api": "people",
      "Locale": "pt-br",
      "Parameters": "doc{CPF}",
      "Datasets": "basic_data.listen(TaxIdStatus)",
      "TargetHook": "email{john@doe.com}"
    }
  ],
  "QueryId": "1cfecda3-7cdf-4c9e-10e7-e66c5b2b74a5",
  "ElapsedMilliseconds": 0,
  "QueryDate": "2024-09-19T13:51:39.898Z",
  "Status": {
    "subscription": [
      {
        "Code": 0,
        "Message": "OK"
      }
    ]
  }
}

Request
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/precos/

This is the basic Pricing API call, which returns the full pricing table associated with the customer, or estimated query prices based on the datasets, API, and country entered. Thus, this call supports two query modes, described below. Each query modality has distinct input parameters and return values. No query performed on the Pricing API has a cost for the customer.

Full Price List Functionality

This functionality returns the complete table of values registered to the client performing the query.

The example below shows how to make a Full Price List call on the Price API via POST:

Body:
{
}


The return contains the complete list of datasets enabled for the customer, and the price tables, with discount ranges, for each of them.

Estimated Query Price Functionality

To request the estimated price of a query, it is necessary to inform some input parameters, described below:

Parameter Name	Description	Possible Values
API	API you want to query	People, Companies, Validations, ...
Datasets	Datasets would be queried in the informed API	Any of the datasets described in the Datasets section
Locale	(Optional) Locality code consulted	PT-BR, ES-MX, ...

The example below shows how to make an Estimated Query Price call in the Pricing API via POST:

Body:
{
  "API": "People",
  "Datasets" : "basic_data, phones, ondemand_rf_status, ondemand_coaf, ondemand_restiuicao, addresses, emails"
}


The return contains the datasets from the list sent in the input that were found (which are enabled for the user and available in this API), and the total price of the query. If any of the datasets informed in the input has not been found in the informed API, but exists in some other one, an indicator of the correct API where the dataset is is returned.

Request
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/log/
Description

The Events API allows you to retrieve log and audit records of queries executed on the Data Platform. Each query generates a record that includes information such as execution time, source IP, parameters used, and the returned result. All query records are stored indefinitely and are available for access via the API for at least 3 months. To obtain older records, please contact us .

Request Parameters

The request must contain a JSON body with the search parameters. It is mandatory to provide one of the following parameters: QueryId, q or Tags.

Parameter	Type	Mandatory	Description
QueryId	string	Conditional	Unique identifier of the query to be retrieved.
q	string	Conditional	Search string to filter events based on the original query parameters. Ex: doc{12345678901}.
Tags	object	Conditional	Object with chave:valor to search queries by custom tags. The search will return events that contain all the tags provided.
QueryDate	string	Optional	Reference date for the query in the format specified in DateFormat. Optimizes search when used with QueryId or filters results when used with q.
DateFormat	string	Optional	Date format reported in QueryDate. Ex: yyyy-MM-dd. Mandatory if QueryDate is informed.
PageSize	integer	Optional	Sets the number of records per page. Used only with the parameter q. Default value: 50.
Cursor	string	Optional	Pointer to the next page of results, obtained from a previous response. Used for pagination in conjunction with q.

Note: We strongly recommend that all QueryIDs be stored, as this is the strongest link between queries and log and audit records. By default, a user has access to all event records associated with their organization.

Examples

Retrieve the result of a specific query faster, with a reference date:

{
    "QueryId": "[seu query id]",
    "QueryDate": "2019-07-25",
    "DateFormat": "yyyy-MM-dd"
}

Retrieve all queries that use specific tags:

{
    "Tags": {
        "Key1": "Value1",
        "Key2": "Value2"
    }
}
Retrieve all queries made against a specific CPF, in sets of 10:
{
    "q": "doc{xxxxxxxxxxx}",
    "PageSize": 10
}
Perform pagination by results:
{
    "q": "doc{xxxxxxxxxxx}",
    "PageSize": 10,
    "Cursor": "[valor de cursor retornado pela chamada anterior]"
}
RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month

Tags
object | null

Tags used in the call

TAGS OBJECT | NULL

string | null

Query date format

Cursor

int32

RESPONSE

https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/tokens/desabilitar

The method Disable allows the user to disable a token he has, passing the token and his access credentials as a parameter to the call.

This method is at no cost to the user.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.

required

success

To generate
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/tokens/gerar

The method To generate allows the creation of access tokens using a username and password. In addition to the user, for which the token will be valid, and the password of that user, the method also receives a parameter expires, which defines the expiration date of the access token that will be created, in hours.

This method is at no cost to the user. The same user can have as many active tokens simultaneously as desired.

Return

In addition to the token generated with the specified parameters (or an error message, if any), the method generate also returns a unique identifier for the token, called tokenID. This identifier can be saved and used later to disable tokens that need to be rotated for some reason.

Companies
POST
Get Details
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/views/obterDetalhes
Description

The Get Details call allows the user to retrieve detailed information about all custom queries to which they have access, or some subset of these custom queries, based on filters. By definition, a user has access to two different scopes of custom queries: the scope of their domain (the group of users associated with their company) and the global scope, which includes all custom queries created by BigData Corp. to simplify the use of data by clients.

All custom queries residing within the domain scope are private and only accessible to users of that domain.

Operation

If no parameters are passed in the Body, all custom queries that the token is allowed to use and that are within the scope of your domain will be returned, regardless of any other characteristics. However, the query also allows the use of other search parameters, which enable filtering custom queries by some of their characteristics. The supported fields are listed below:

Scope: Defines the scope of the custom queries that will be returned. It can receive the value global or domain;

View: Defines the name of the custom query being searched within the selected scope. If specified, the found custom query will be returned, or no results will be displayed if no custom query with the specified name exists.

API: Defines the API to which the custom queries being searched refer. It can receive the value "people" or "companies";

These filters allow for the quick identification of customized queries for your management or direct use.

Examples

How to retrieve available global custom queries:

POST https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/views/obterDetalhes
Body:
{
	"Scope": "global"
}


How to retrieve custom queries that use the People API process dataset:

POST https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/views/obterDetalhes
Body:
{
	"Scope": "global",
	"Api": "people",
	"Datasets": "processes"
}


How to retrieve a specific custom query:

POST https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/views/obterDetalhes
Body:
{
	"View": "[nome da sua consulta personalizada]"
}

Authentication and security

Ensure secure, scalable, and traceable integrations, preserving data integrity and the reliability of your applications.

Authentication is the starting point for any integration with BigDataCorp products – and security is a fundamental part of this process.

In this section, you will find everything you need to securely access APIs, manage your authentication tokens, and apply best practices for protection and rotation.

Here you will learn how to:

Generate and renew your JWT tokens directly through the BDC Center or via the Token API;
Properly configure the authentication headers in your requests;
Implement security and traceability policies in test and production environments;
Adopt best practices for storing and using credentials .

Our goal is to ensure that your integrations are secure, scalable, and traceable, preserving data integrity and the reliability of your applications.



💡

BigDataCorp's APIs use JWT tokens for authentication. These ensure security, access control, and flexibility in your integrations.



🔐Authentication

If you are not yet a BigDataCorp customer, please contact our sales team at comercial@bigdatacorp.com.br . They will assist you with registration and obtaining your access credentials.

If you are already a customer, access the BDC Center . There, you can change or reset your password at any time.

BigDataCorp API authentication is done using JWT tokens . With your username and password, you can generate as many tokens as you need, ensuring security and flexibility for your business processes.

Tokens can be generated directly in the BDC Center or via the Token API . It's important to remember that all tokens have an expiration date—there are no permanent tokens.

The Token API is available at no additional cost, allowing your company to implement a token rotation policy that better suits your needs. To learn more about recommendations and best practices, see the Security section.



⚠️

Attention: Your access token is unique and should not be shared. You will be responsible for the cost of all queries made using your access token.



Authentication framework

All calls to the BigDataCorp Data Platform follow the same authentication pattern.

Endpoint base:

https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/

Required headers:

AccessToken: SEU-ACCESS-TOKEN
TokenId: SEU-TOKENID
Content-Type: application/json
Accept: application/json

These tokens can be obtained from the BDC Center or via the Tokens API .



💡
Tip

The authentication format is the same for all APIs on the BigDataCorp Data Platform . Simply adjust the endpoint according to the desired entity . For example:

• .../pessoas → calls to datasets of People
• .../empresas → calls to datasets of Companies



🛡️Security


Usernames and passwords

User and password management is handled by the BDC Center . We recommend that each user have their own account and that credentials are never shared .

When integrating our APIs into your systems, use dedicated service users, always associated with valid corporate email addresses.

In production environments, generate and use tokens linked to these service users, ensuring traceability and security in the use of APIs.



Token Rotation

Although the Token API allows tokens to be generated with a validity of up to 10 years (87,600 hours), we suggest rotating tokens daily . We do not recommend rotating tokens more than once within the same hour.

Consult the Tokens API documentation — it offers resources that allow you to implement the token rotation policy best suited to your organization.



Tokens and web applications

Use tokens only in applications that operate on the backend . They function as your authentication for BigDataCorp APIs and can be exposed or compromised if used directly on the application's frontend .



Access restriction

If your architecture requires additional security layers, you can request the configuration of access restrictions to the Data Platform APIs, limiting calls to specific domains (CORS) or authorized IP ranges .

To enable any of these settings, contact your Customer Success representative, informing them of the domain that needs to be authorized (CORS) or the allowed IP ranges .



Request limit

For security and platform stability reasons, the APIs have a rate limit of 5,000 queries every 5 minutes (per IP address) . Requests should be distributed throughout this interval, avoiding call spikes in short periods.

If the limit is exceeded, the API will return an error. HTTP 429 — Too Many Requests.




Updated about 1 month ago

Welcome

Begin your integration journey with BigDataCorp. Here, you'll find instructions, examples, and best practices for connecting to our products and APIs.

👋 Welcome to the BigDataCorp technical documentation!

Here you'll find everything you need to get started — and grow — with BigDataCorp products. Our goal is to provide clear instructions, usage examples, and best practices to ensure secure, scalable, and efficient integrations.

In this documentation, you will be able to:

To become familiar with the datasets on the BigDataCorp Data Platform .
Understanding how API calls, parameters, and responses work.
Consult detailed instructions regarding authentication and security, as well as status codes .
Follow the integration examples available in Getting Started .
Explore the endpoints of each entity and their respective datasets.


🔎 How to navigate

The documentation has been organized to make navigation simple and intuitive. Use the side menu to quickly find what you need:

Overview: Presents the main concepts and guidelines on Authentication and Security and Status Codes .
First Steps: provides a quick and practical guide to get you started with your integration, covering prerequisites, token generation, and your first API call.
API navigation: explore the specific endpoints for each entity (person, company, product, etc.), organized by dataset sets, with usage examples and detailed parameters.


Happy exploring! 🚀

Take the opportunity to explore our datasets and discover how BigDataCorp's data can boost your solutions.

Status codes and descriptions

Consult the complete list of status codes and descriptions to understand what each return represents.

Status codes and descriptions are responses from BigDataCorp APIs that indicate the result of processing a request.

They allow you to identify whether the call was successful, whether there was an error, or whether some exceptional condition occurred during processing.

In all calls to the BigDataCorp Data Platform, the response includes a field. status, which describes the result of the operation and assists in diagnosing integrations.



❗️

Each status code corresponds to a specific type of return: success, authentication error, communication failure, or other conditions handled during processing.

Full list

Below you will find the complete list of status codes returned by the BigDataCorp Data Platform, organized into groups for easier navigation.

All sections below are within expandable blocks. You can navigate by expanding each group or by using your browser's search function.

💡

Tip: To find a specific code on this page, use Ctrl + F (or ⌘ + F on a Mac).



Input data

Errors related to parameters, permissions, views, datasets, or query syntax..

(-100 to -999)
Login problems

Errors related to authentication, session, or token binding.

(-1000 to -1199)
Internal problems

Internal platform errors, timeouts, infrastructure failures, or temporary unavailability.

(-1200 to -1999)
On-demand consultations

Errors related to external sources, CAPTCHA, crawling, or limitations on data returned by the source.

(-2000 to -2999)
Monitoring or asynchronous calls

Errors related to subscriptions, hooks, batch subscribe, and other asynchronous platform operations.

(-3000 onwards)


📘

If you've encountered a code not listed here or need additional support interpreting a return, please contact your Customer Success representative.




Updated 27 days ago

https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset aggregates indicators related to companies' social engagement, including aspects associated with accessibility, workforce diversity, and the relationship between the highest and lowest salaries, with metrics derived from inference models applied to public or publicly accessible information.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the ESG documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: syndicate_agreements.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month
BODY PARAMS
q
string
required

A string that defines the query's input parameters, used to specify the identifiers and filters applicable to the dataset.

Datasets
string
required

String containing the technical names of the datasets to be queried.

Limit
number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

RESPONSE
200

Dataset return parameters.

RESPONSE BODY
object
Result
array of objects
required

Result lists

object
SyndicateAgreements
object
required

The main object returned by the dataset, containing consolidated information related to the queried entity.

SYNDICATEAGREEMENTS OBJECT
QueryId
string
required

Unique caller ID.

ElapsedMilliseconds
number
required

The number of milliseconds elapsed during the call.

QueryDate
string
required

The date when the request was received and processed.

Status
object
required

Status list for each dataset.

VIEW ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES
Evidences
object
required

List of evidence used for each dataset.

VIEW ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES

Online Ads
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset gathers information about online advertisements associated with the company being consulted, based on the identification of seller profiles on portals and marketplaces focused on buying and selling between individuals. It includes consolidated data from the identified profiles and metrics related to advertising activity, and there may be more than one profile associated with the same company.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Digital Presence documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: online_ads.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Possible Values
activeads	Minimum number of active ads.	Any integer
totalads	Minimum total number of ads published.	Any integer
admaxvalue	Minimum maximum value among the identified ads.	Any decimal number
adminvalue	Minimum value among the identified ads.	Any decimal number
portal	The portal where the ads were published.	Any portal name
category	Category associated with published ads.	Any category name
Limit

This dataset allows you to limit the number of records returned using the modifier .limit(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history

Make a request to see history.

Limit

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

Reputation and Reviews
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset gathers information about a company's reputation across various service review platforms, presenting indicators by source, a consolidated view of its reputation, and a historical overview of its evolution over time. This data supports risk analysis, company valuation, and can be used as input in analytical and statistical models.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Reputation documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: reputations_and_reviews.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.090
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.085
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.081
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.077
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.073
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 61,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Social Conscience
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset aggregates indicators related to companies' social engagement, including aspects associated with accessibility, workforce diversity, and the relationship between the highest and lowest salaries, with metrics derived from inference models applied to public or publicly accessible information.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the ESG documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: social_conscience.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Merchant Category Data - MCC
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset returns the business categorization of the queried company based on the MCC (Merchant Category Code), a four-digit numeric code used in the payment processing ecosystem to classify companies according to the type of goods or services offered. The return may include direct MCC associations when available, as well as associations inferred from the company's CNAE codes.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Basic Data documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: merchant_category_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Owners Lawsuits Distribution Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset gathers aggregated data on the distribution of legal proceedings involving the partners of the consulted entity, consolidating indicators derived from the main fields of the Legal Proceedings dataset. The result provides a summary view of the volume and nature of the proceedings associated with the partners, facilitating preliminary analyses without the need to evaluate individual records on a large scale.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Legal Proceedings documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: owners_lawsuits_distribution_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.100
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.096
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.092
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.088
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.084
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	R$68,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
capturedate	Process capture date	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
closedate	Process closing date	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
cnjsubject	Number or name of the subject in the CNJ (National Council of Justice) inferred.	Equal	Number or text
cnjproceduretype	Inferred process number or type name in the CNJ (National Council of Justice).	Equal	Number or text
courtlevel	Court level	Equal	1, 2, 3
courtname	Court name	Equal	STF, STJ, TRF1, JFAC, JFAM, JFAP, JFBA, JFDF, JFGO, JFMA, JFMG, JFMT, JFPA, JFPI, JFRO, JFRR, JFTO, TRF2, JFES, JFRJ, TRF3, JFMS, JFSP, TRF4, JFPR, JFRS, JFSC, TRF5, JFAL, JFCE, JFPB, JFPE, JFRN, JFSE, TRF6, TJAC, TJAL, TJAM, TJAP, TJBA, TJCE, TJDF, TJES, TJGO, TJMA, TJMG, TJMS, TJMT, TJPA, TJPB, TJPE, TJPI, TJPR, TJRJ, TJRN, TJRO, TJRR, TJRS, TJSC, TJSE, TJSP, TJTO, TRT1, TRT2, TRT3, TRT4, TRT5, TRT6, TRT7, TRT8, TRT9, TRT10, TRT11, TRT12, TRT13, TRT14, TRT15, ​​TRT16, TRT17, TRT18, TRT19, TRT20, TRT21, TRT22, TRT23, TRT24, TST, TRE-AC, TRE-AL, TRE-AM, TRE-AP, TRE-BA, TRE-CE, TRE-DF, TRE-ES, TRE-GO, TRE-MA, TRE-MG, TRE-MS, TRE-MT, TRE-PA, TRE-PB, TRE-PE, TRE-PI, TRE-PR, TRE-RJ, TRE-RN, TRE-RO, TRE-RR, TRE-RS, TRE-SC, TRE-SE, TRE-SP, TRE-TO, TSE
courttype	Court type	Equal	CIVIL, CRIMINAL, TAX, SOCIAL SECURITY, LABOR, SPECIAL CIVIL, SPECIAL CRIMINAL, ADMINISTRATIVE, ELECTORAL, FINANCE, SPECIAL FINANCE
partypolarity	Part polarity	Equal	ACTIVE, PASSIVE, NEUTRAL
partytype	Part type	Equal	AUTHOR, DEFENDANT, CLAIMANT, CLAIMED, LAWYER, REPORTER, ATTORNEY, WITNESS, VICTIM, JUDGE, INMATE, OTHER
noticed	Process notification date	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
state	State	Equal	RJ, SP, MG, ES, etc.
status	Process status	Contains	ACTIVE, DISTRIBUTED, DOWNLOADED, APPENDED, DECIDED, FILED, EXECUTION, FINALIZED, CLOSED, AT APPEAL, REDISTRIBUTED, FINAL, FILED, JUDGED, INDEFINITE
resjudicatadate	Res Judicata Date	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
value	Amount disputed in the process	Between	[minimum, maximum]

POST
Lawsuits Distribution Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset consolidates aggregated information about legal proceedings in which the consulted entity is involved, allowing for a quick overview of the distribution of these proceedings across different dimensions, such as type, court, state, status, and party role. The return is based on the main fields of the Legal Proceedings dataset, making it suitable for exploratory analyses and preliminary assessments, without the need to consume large volumes of detailed data. Distribution values ​​are aggregated in real time, prioritizing the most recent data. In cases of entities with more than 500,000 proceedings, the return may be empty, accompanied by an error. UNABLE TO AGGREGATE DATA IN TIME (code -1215).

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Legal Proceedings documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: lawsuits_distribution_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing you to refine the query or change how the data is analyzed and returned.

Key	Description	Possible Values
alternativedistributionparameter	By specifying this parameter, an additional object will be returned in the dataset output, which refers to the aggregation based on the values ​​of the field specified in this parameter.	PartyType

When the supplementary key alternativedistributionparameter If the value is specified, the dataset includes an additional block of aggregations in the return, calculated from the values ​​found for the indicated field (for example, PartyTypeThis additional return is available within the object. LawsuitsDistributionByAlternativeParameterValues, which relates each identified value of the alternative parameter (e.g., APPLICANT, RESPONDENT, PLAINTIFF) to its respective distributions for the same aggregated dimensions of the main return (such as process type, court, status, state, and other distributions).

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
capturedate	Process capture date	Between (Requires two values)	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
closedate	Process closing date	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
cnjsubject	Subject number or name in the CNJ. When numeric values are used, this filter applies to the InferredCNJSubjectNumber and InferredBroadCNJSubjectNumber fields. In case of text values, it applies to the InferredCNJSubjectName and InferredBroadCNJSubjectName fields.	Equal	Number or text
cnjproceduretype	Number or name of the process type in the CNJ. When numbers are entered, the filter is applied to the InferredCNJProcedureTypeNumber field, and if they are texts, it is applied to the InferredCNJProcedureTypeName field.	Equal	Number or text
courtlevel	Court level	Equal	1, 2, 3
courtname	Name of Court	Equal	STF, STJ, TRF1, JFAC, JFAM, JFAP, JFBA, JFDF, JFGO, JFMA, JFMG, JFMT, JFPA, JFPI, JFRO, JFRR, JFTO, TRF2, JFES, JFRJ, TRF3, JFMS, JFSP, TRF4, JFPR, JFRS, JFSC, TRF5, JFAL, JFCE, JFPB, JFPE, JFRN, JFSE, TRF6, TJAC, TJAL, TJAM, TJAP, TJBA, TJCE, TJDF, TJES, TJGO, TJMA, TJMG, TJMS, TJMT, TJPA, TJPB, TJPE, TJPI, TJPR, TJRJ, TJRN, TJRO, TJRR, TJRS, TJSC, TJSE, TJSP, TJTO, TRT1, TRT2, TRT3, TRT4, TRT5, TRT6, TRT7, TRT8, TRT9, TRT10, TRT11, TRT12, TRT13, TRT14, TRT15, ​​TRT16, TRT17, TRT18, TRT19, TRT20, TRT21, TRT22, TRT23, TRT24, TST, TRE-AC, TRE-AL, TRE-AM, TRE-AP, TRE-BA, TRE-CE, TRE- DF, TRE-ES, TRE-GO, TRE-MA, TRE-MG, TRE-MS, TRE-MT, TRE-PA, TRE-PB, TRE-PE, TRE-PI, TRE-PR, TRE-RJ, TRE-RN, TRE-RO, TRE-RR, TRE-RS, TRE-SC, TRE-SE, TRE-SP, TRE-TO, TSE.
courttype	Court Type	Equal	CIVIL, CRIMINAL, TAX, SOCIAL SECURITY, LABOR, SPECIAL CIVIL, SPECIAL CRIMINAL, ADMINISTRATIVE, ELECTORAL, FINANCE, SPECIAL FINANCE
partypolarity	Part polarity	Equal	ACTIVE, PASSIVE, NEUTRAL
partytype	Part type	Equal	AUTHOR, DEFENDANT, CLAIMANT, CLAIMED, LAWYER, REPORTER, ATTORNEY, WITNESS, VICTIM, JUDGE, INMATE, OTHER
noticed	Process notification date	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
state	State	Equal	RJ, SP, MG, ES, etc.
status	Process status	Contains	ACTIVE, DISTRIBUTED, DOWNLOADED, APPENDED, DECIDED, FILED, EXECUTION, FINALIZED, CLOSED, AT APPEAL, REDISTRIBUTED, FINAL, FILED, JUDGED, INDEFINITE
resjudicatadate	Date of Res Judicata	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
value	Amount disputed in the process	Between	[Minimum maximum]

To understand how to apply filters, sorting, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Investment Funds Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset compiles registration and operational information on investment funds associated with the consulted entity, according to records available from the Brazilian Securities and Exchange Commission (CVM). The return includes basic fund data and consolidated transaction information, when available, allowing for sectoral analyses related to the company's performance in the investment market.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Sectoral documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: investment_fund_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing you to refine the query or change how the data is analyzed and returned.

Key	Description	Possible Values
all	This determines whether all captured transactions for the CNPJ (Brazilian tax ID) should be returned, potentially directly impacting the volume and performance of the response.	true, false (Default: false)
Limit

This dataset allows you to limit the number of records returned using the modifier .limit(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Civil Works Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset compiles registration information on construction projects linked to the consulted entity, based on records available in the National Construction Project Registry (CNO). The results include essential data for identifying and monitoring the projects, such as the CNO number, address, constructed area, and registration status, supporting sectoral analyses and compliance with regulatory and tax obligations.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Sectoral documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: civil_construction_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Registration Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset compiles registration and contact information associated with the company being consulted, with data typically required in registration, validation, and business relationship processes. The return includes basic identification and status information of the company, as well as records of emails, phone numbers, and addresses available from the sources.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Contacts documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: registration_data.

Price list

1 - 10.000	BRL 0.120
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.114
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.108
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.103
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.098
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 79,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Website Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset gathers historical and aggregated information about websites associated with the consulted entity, considering recurring captures made over time, including metrics on the number of related websites and domains, as well as associations between entities, domains, and, in the case of companies, websites linked to partners. The return considers websites as distinct units, meaning a single domain can contain multiple websites and a single website can be distributed across different domains.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Digital Presence documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: domains_extended.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Possible Values
hasssl	Indicates whether the website has an SSL certificate.	true, false
isactive	Indicates whether the site is active.	true, false
ismaindomain	Indicates whether the site belongs to the main domain.	true, false
socialnetworks	Social media networks associated with the website.	facebook, twitter, linkedin, etc.
ispname	Website hosting provider.	Provider name
paymentmethods	Payment methods identified on the website.	credit card, invoice, etc.
paymentservices	Payment services used.	paypal, pagseguro, etc.
framework	Framework or platform used on the website.	Platform name
visitor range	Estimated range of monthly visitors.	Access lane
domainclass	Domain class associated with the website.	PORTAL, BLOG, ECOMMERCE, CORPORATE, GOVERNMENT, NEWS, EDUCATIONAL
IsRegisteredInWhoIsData	Indicates whether the website is registered in the entity's name.	true, false
Limit

This dataset allows you to limit the number of records returned using the modifier .limit(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month

Limit

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

Dataset return parameters.

Government Debtors
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset compiles information on taxpayers registered as principal debtors in outstanding debts to the Federal Government and the FGTS (Brazilian Severance Indemnity Fund), according to the most recent records made available by the Attorney General's Office of the National Treasury (PGFN). The return details the outstanding debts associated with the consulted entity, serving as a more agile alternative to on-demand certificate requests, with the caveat that, since it is periodic data, the information may not reflect very recent updates.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Risk documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: government_debtors.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Owners Electoral Donors
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset gathers information on donations made to election campaigns by members linked to the consulted CNPJ (Brazilian tax ID), including individual donation records and aggregate indicators related to the quantity and total value donated, allowing for analyses of indirect political involvement and associations between entities, members, and political agents.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Political Engagement documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: owners_electoral_donors.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.162
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.154
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.146
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.139
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.132
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 61,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset compiles information on donations made to election campaigns by the consulted entity, including individual donation records and aggregate indicators related to the quantity and total value donated, allowing for analyses of political involvement and associations between entities and political agents throughout the considered election cycles.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Political Engagement documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: electoral_donors.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Companies
POST
Emails from Related People
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset compiles emails associated with individuals related to the company being consulted, considering links such as partners, administrators, employees, and other types of relationships identified in the people and companies graph. The result includes a list of emails linked to these individuals, along with attributes describing the type of relationship, email characteristics, validation status, recency, and priority, supporting contact, relationship, and risk analyses.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Contacts documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: related_people_emails.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
relationshiptype	Type of relationship the person has with the company.	Equal	QSA, Ownership, Employee, Legal Representative
type	Email type	Equal	corporate, personal
isactive	Indicates whether the email is active.	Equal	true, false
isrecent	Indicates whether the email is recent.	Equal	true, false
ismain	Indicates whether the email is the primary one.	Equal	true, false
validationstatus	Email validation status	Equal	VALID, ACCEPT_ALL, UNKNOWN, POSSIBLE_SPAM_TRAP, INVALID, HARDBOUNCE, RISKY, ACCEPT
domain	Email domain	Equal	gmail.com, yahoo.com.br, ...
Limit

This dataset allows you to limit the number of records returned using the modifier .limit(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month
BODY PARAMS
q
string
required

A string that defines the query's input parameters, used to specify the identifiers and filters applicable to the dataset.

Datasets
string
required

String containing the technical names of the datasets to be queried.

Limit
number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

RESPONSE
200

Dataset return parameters.

RESPONSE BODY
object
Result
array of objects
required

Result lists

object
RelatedPeopleEmails
array of objects
required
object
RelationshipType
string
required

Indicates the type of relationship associated with this record. (QSA, Ownership, Employee, Legal Representative)

EmailAddress
string
required

Email address

Domain
string
required

Email address domain (after the '@' symbol).

UserName
string
required

Username (before the '@' symbol).

Type
string
required

Composite field that describes the related entity and the email context. (RELATED - {DOCUMENT-NUMBER} - {RELATIONSHIP-TYPE} - {EMAIL-TYPE (CORPORATE or PERSONAL)})

EmailTotalPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email has been viewed for this entity.

EmailBadPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email was seen for this entity in a suspicious situation.

EmailCrawlingPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email was seen for this entity by a crawling or capturing process.

EmailValidationPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email has been seen for this entity through a validation process.

EmailQueryPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email was seen by this entity in a query by query.

EmailMonthAveragePassages
integer
required

Average monthly number of times this email was viewed by this entity.

EmailNumberOfEntities
integer
required

Number of different entities associated with this email.

Last3MonthsPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email has been viewed by this entity in the last 3 months.

Last6MonthsPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email has been viewed by this entity in the last 6 months.

Last12MonthsPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email has been viewed by this entity in the last 12 months.

Last18MonthsPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email has been viewed by this entity in the last 18 months.

Priority
integer
required

Priority level of this email for this entity.

IsMain
boolean
required

Indicates whether this is the entity's primary email address.

IsRecent
boolean
required

Indicates whether this email is recent for this entity.

IsActive
boolean
required

Indicates whether this email is active for this entity.

ValidationStatus
string
required

The last recorded validation status of the email validation process. (VALID, ACCEPT_ALL, UNKNOWN, POSSIBLE_SPAM_TRAP, INVALID, HARDBOUNCE, RISKY, ACCEPT)

LastValidationDate
string
required

Date the email was last validated.

FirstPassageDate
string
required

Date this email was first seen for this entity.

LastPassageDate
string
required

Date this email was last seen for this entity.

CreationDate
string
required

Date the record was created.

LastUpdateDate
string
required

Date the record was last updated.

QueryId
string
required

Unique caller ID.

ElapsedMilliseconds
number
required

The number of milliseconds elapsed during the call.

QueryDate
string
required

The date when the request was received and processed.

Status
object
required

Status list for each dataset.

VIEW ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES
Evidences
object
required

List of evidence used for each dataset.

VIEW ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES

Emails
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/empresas
Description

This dataset gathers information on emails associated with the company being consulted, including individual characteristics of each address and aggregated indicators about the set of identified emails. The results allow for the evaluation of attributes such as email type, validation status, recency, priority, and volume of occurrences, supporting contact analysis, database quality, risk assessment, and data modeling.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Contacts documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: emails_extended.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing you to refine the query or change how the data is analyzed and returned.

Key	Description	Possible Values
e-mail	Allows you to provide an additional email address for analysis of global address information, regardless of direct association with the CNPJ (Brazilian company tax ID) consulted.	An email address
returnonlydifferentemails	This specifies that only email addresses different from those specified in the query should be returned.	true, false
returnonlyvalidemails	It specifies that only emails with a validation status considered valid should be returned.	true, false

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
Type	Email type	Equal	corporate, personal
isactive	Indicates whether the email is active.	Equal	true, false
isrecent	Indicates whether the email is recent.	Equal	true, false
ismain	Indicates whether the email is the primary one.	Equal	true, false
validationstatus	Email validation status	Equal	VALID, ACCEPT_ALL, UNKNOWN, POSSIBLE_SPAM_TRAP, INVALID, HARDBOUNCE, RISKY, ACCEPT
domain	Email domain	Equal	gmail.com, yahoo.com.br, ...
Limit

This dataset allows you to limit the number of records returned using the modifier .limit(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
