Indigenous Lands
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/addresses
Description

This dataset identifies the proximity, intersection, or insertion of an address, point, or geographic area in relation to Indigenous lands, returning information associated with Indigenous lands related to the analyzed area. The data provided supports risk analysis, territorial compliance, and validation of activities subject to legal and socio-environmental restrictions.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Environmental and Cultural Protection documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: indigenous_lands_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing for the definition of different forms of georeferencing or direct reference to the analyzed indigenous lands.

Key	Description	Possible Values
polygon	Geographic polygon used to delimit the area of ​​analysis.	List of longitude/latitude coordinates
householdcode	Geographic residence identifier	Code
building code	Geographic identifier for a building.	Code
latitude + longitude	Coordinates of the lookout point (must be provided together)	latitude{NUMBER}, longitude{NUMBER}

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month
BODY PARAMS
q
string
required

A string that defines the query's input parameters, used to specify the identifiers and filters applicable to the dataset.

Datasets
string
required

String containing the technical names of the datasets to be queried.

Limit
number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

RESPONSE
200

Dataset return parameters.

RESPONSE BODY
object

Conservation units
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/addresses
Description

This dataset identifies the proximity, intersection, or insertion of an address, point, or geographic area in relation to conservation units, returning information associated with the units related to the analyzed area. The data provided supports risk analyses, environmental compliance, and validation of activities subject to legal and territorial restrictions.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Environmental and Cultural Protection documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: conservation_units.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing for the definition of different ways to georeference the analyzed area.

Key	Description	Possible Values
polygon	Geographic polygon used to delimit the area of ​​analysis.	List of longitude/latitude coordinates
householdcode	Geographic residence identifier	Code
building code	Geographic identifier for a building.	Code
latitude + longitude	Coordinates of the lookout point (must be provided together)	latitude{NUMBER}, longitude{NUMBER}

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month
BODY PARAMS
q
string
required

A string that defines the query's input parameters, used to specify the identifiers and filters applicable to the dataset.

Datasets
string
required

String containing the technical names of the datasets to be queried.

Limit
number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

RESPONSE
200

Dataset return parameters.

RESPONSE BODY
object
Result
array of objects
required

Result lists

object
Conservation Units
object
required

The main object returned by the dataset, containing consolidated information related to the queried entity.

CONSERVATIONUNITS OBJECT
QueryId
string
required

Unique caller ID.

ElapsedMilliseconds
number
required

The number of milliseconds elapsed during the call.

QueryDate
string
required

The date when the request was received and processed.

Status
object
required

Status list for each dataset.

VIEW ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES

object
required

List of evidence used for each dataset.

Agroecological Zoning
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/addresses
Description

Dataset que retorna informações sobre o zoneamento agroecológico da região consultada, incluindo indicações de aptidão e restrições para diferentes usos agropecuários, como cultivo agrícola, pastagem e pecuária, conforme os dados oficiais disponíveis para a área analisada.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Environmental and Cultural Protection documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: sugarcane_agroecological_zoning.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.090
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.085
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.081
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.077
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.073
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 61,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing for the definition of different ways to georeference the analyzed area.

Key	Description	Possible Values
householdcode	Geographic residence identifier	Code
building code	Geographic identifier for a building.	Code
state	Unidade federativa utilizada como referência geográfica	State acronym
cities	Lista de municípios utilizados na consulta	List of city names
city	Município utilizado como referência geográfica	City's name
geocode	Código geográfico definido pelo IBGE	Código IBGE
polygon	Geographic polygon used to delimit the area of ​​analysis.	List of longitude/latitude coordinates

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month
BODY PARAMS
q
string
required

A string that defines the query's input parameters, used to specify the identifiers and filters applicable to the dataset.

Datasets
string
required

String containing the technical names of the datasets to be queried.

Limit
number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

RESPONSE
200

Dataset return parameters.

RESPONSE BODY
object
Result
array of objects
required

Result lists

object
SugarcaneZoningData
object
required

The main object returned by the dataset, containing consolidated information related to the queried entity.

SUGARCANEZONINGDATA OBJECT
QueryId
string
required

Unique caller ID.

ElapsedMilliseconds
number
required

The number of milliseconds elapsed during the call.

QueryDate
string
required

The date when the request was received and processed.

Status
object
required

Status list for each dataset.

VIEW ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES
Evidences
object
required

List of evidence used for each dataset.

Consultation structure

Now that you've made your first query, let's explore the standard structure of requests sent to the BigDataCorp Data Platform.

This page was created to help you understand. how the queries are structured on the BigDataCorp Data Platform.
Here you will learn how to correctly organize parameters in the request body, understand the role of each one, and build consistent, reusable queries aligned with the standard adopted in all APIs of the platform.

By the end of this section, you will have a complete overview of the basic structure of a query, which will serve as a starting point for exploring, in the following pages, the available parameters, identification keys, customization of the return, and additional features.

Basic structure of the request

The Data Platform APIs use the POST method, receiving three main parameters in the request body:

q – search keys (mandatory)
Datasets - desired datasets in the return (mandatory)
Limit – maximum limit of entities returned (optional)


Query using primary key (CPF)
JSON
{
  "q": "doc{12345678900}",
  "Datasets": "basic_data"
}
Query using alternative keys and Limit
JSON
{
  "q": "name{josé da silva}, birthdate{01-01-1970}, dateformat{dd-mm-yyyy}",
  "Datasets": "basic_data",
  "Limit": 5
}

This structure is used in all APIs of the Data Platform (people, companies, addresses, processes, products, vehicles, etc.).

Next steps

After understanding the basic structure of the query, you can move on to:

Query parameters, where you will find all mandatory and optional parameters, including primary, alternative and optional keys, their concepts, explanations and practical examples.
Customize the results, where you will learn how to select specific fields, apply filters, and sort the returned data.
Additional resources, where you'll see how to send a hashed document and how to add tags to calls to track internal flows or operations.

Request Parameters

A guide to the parameters that structure a query on the Platform.

The parameters structure the query and guide the Data Platform on who to locate and what set of information to return .

Here you will learn:

The function of each parameter (q, Datasets, Limit)
How to declare identification keys
The differences between main, alternative, and optional keys.
When and how to combine keys
Complete lists of keys, by type
When to use Limit

These concepts are essential for the following pages to make sense.

1️⃣ Available parameters
Parameter	Description	Type
q	List of search keys for the query, separated by commas.	🛑 Mandatory
Datasets	List of returned datasets, which may include modifiers.	🛑 Mandatory
Limit	Maximum limit of entities returned.	ℹ️ Optional
2️⃣ Regarding the parameter q

The parameter q brings together identification keys sent to locate an entity on the Data Platform.
It functions like the "who am I trying to find" within your search.

Each key follows the format: nome_da_chave{valor}.

Simple example
JSON
"q": "name{Maria Souza}"


❗️
Sending q It is mandatory in all consultations.
🔑 Identification keys

The Data Platform uses three types of identification keys:

Master keys → uniquely identify the entity (1:1)
Alternative keys → can return more than one entity.
Additional keywords → refine the search and increase precision.

Next, we detail each group.

Key Keys

These are identifiers that have a 1:1 relationship with the entity being consulted.

Main Key	Description	Example	API
doc	Individual or Company Identification Number (CPF/CNPJ)	doc{12345678900}	Person or Company
zipcode	Postal Code	zipcode{12345000}	Address
licenseplate	Plate number	licenseplate{ABC1D23}	Vehicle
processnumber	Process number	processnumber{10000000120258260302}	Process
receiptnumber	Invoice Number	receiptnumber{123456789}	Invoice
ean	Barcode Number	ean{12345678890123}	Product
Alternative keys

When the user does not have a primary key, they must submit at least one alternate key. Because they are less specific, it is recommended to submit two or more alternate keys, or combine them with supplementary keys.

Alternative Key	Description	Example	API
name	Entity name	name{josé da silva}	Person or Company
classnumber + classorganization	Registration number with the professional council and name of the organization.	classnumber{123456}, classorganization{CFMSP}	Person
nit	Employee Registration Number (or ID number)	nit{123456789}	Person
rntrc	ANTT Registration Number	rntrc{12345678}	Person or Company
domain	Domain (website URL)	domain{seudominio.com}	Person or Company
name + partialdoc	Name and partial information from the entity's identification document.	name{big corp}, partialdoc{*8768627*}	Person or Company
Additional keys

Complementary keys do not identify on their own, but they help reduce ambiguities, increase accuracy, and complement alternative keys.

Additional keys (click to expand)


💡
Tip:

The more alternative and supplementary keys you send, the greater the chances of reaching the correct entity .

❗️
Attention:

In certain datasets, keys classified as complementary may become mandatory due to specific requirements of the data sources.

When this occurs, the requirement will be explicitly indicated in the "Relevant supplementary keys" section of the respective dataset.

3️⃣ Regarding the parameter Datasets

The parameter Datasets informs which datasets These should be returned by the API. It functions as "what do I want to receive from this entity".

Examples of common datasets:

basic_data
kyc
processes

The complete list of datasets can be found in the specific documentation for each API, in the sidebar menu.

THE Datasets It also allows:

field selection
use of modifiers
Composing multiple datasets in the same call

These features are detailed on the “Customize return” page.

4️⃣ Regarding the parameter Limit

THE Limit controls maximum number of entities Errors returned when the query is not specific enough.

Use when:

if there is no master key
the query could generate multiple occurrences
If you want to limit the number of entities returned


Example
JSON
{
  "Datasets": "basic_data",
  "q": "name{Joao da Silva}, birthdate{10-02-1995}, dateformat{dd-mm-yyyy}",
  "Limit": 5
}
5️⃣ Practical Summary
q → Who am I looking for?
Datasets → what I want to receive
Limit → Maximum number of entities I want to receive when the search key is not 1:1

These three parameters form the basis of all queries on the Data Platform.

Next steps

Now that you understand all the query parameters, proceed to:

Customize the results – selecting fields, applying filters, and sorting.
Additional resources – use of dochash, tags, and complementary functionalities.


✨ From here, you've mastered the logic of requests. Now it's just a matter of exploring how to refine and enrich your responses.



Updated 27 days ago

Consultation structure
Customize return
Did this page help you?
Yes
At the
1️⃣ Available parameters
2️⃣ Regarding the q parameter
🔑 Identification keys
3️⃣ Regarding the Datasets parameter
4️⃣ Regarding the Limit parameter
5️⃣ Practical Summary
Next steps
 English

Customize return

Personalize the results of your queries by selecting fields, applying filters, controlling sorting, paginating results, limiting results, and using a reference date .

The resources on this page allow you to refine the response from the Data Platform APIs, delivering only the information necessary for your workflow.

Here you will learn how to:

Select specific fields from a dataset .
Filter returned records
Sorting lists of data
Paginate results
Limit the number of results
Use reference date

These features help reduce the volume of data transferred, improve the performance of integrations, and make the responses more aligned with your needs.

1️⃣ Select fields from a dataset

In some scenarios, it is not necessary to receive all available fields in a dataset.
For this, it is possible to provide this information at the time of the call. which fields should be returned, directly in the parameter Datasets.

Field selection is done by specifying the dataset name followed by the desired fields in curly braces, separated by commas ({campo1, campo2}).

Examples:

Selecting fields in a dataset
JSON
Body:
{
    "Datasets": "basic_data{name, birthdate, taxidstatus, mothername}",
    "q": "doc{12345678900}"
}
Multiple datasets with selection in only one of them.

When the call includes more than one dataset, the field selection can be applied only to the desired one:

JSON
Body:
{
    "Datasets": "basic_data{name, birthdate, taxidstatus, mothername}, kyc, processes",
    "q": "doc{12345678900}"
}
❗️
In queries to the On-demand API, it is not possible to select fields to be returned.
2️⃣ Filter returned results

Some datasets allow you to filter the returned results, selecting only information that meets specific criteria.

The filtering rules follow the principles below:

There is no distinction between uppercase and lowercase letters.
It is possible to filter by multiple values ​​in the same field.
It is possible to apply filters to more than one field simultaneously.
Simple filter
JSON
{
    "Datasets": "processes.filter(party_type=defendant)",
    "q": "doc{12345678900}"
}
Filter with multiple values
JSON
{
    "Datasets": "phones.filter(areacode=[21,11])",
    "q": "doc{12345678900}"
}
Filters across multiple fields
JSON
{
    "Datasets": "emails.filter(Type=work,validationstatus=VALID)",
    "q": "doc{12345678900}"
}
📘
To find out if a specific dataset supports filters, consult its documentation.
3️⃣ Sort results lists

Several datasets return lists of results, such as in the cases of contacts (phone numbers, addresses, and emails) and relationships.

By default, these lists are returned following an internal prioritization order, which considers relevance criteria according to the data context.

If it becomes necessary to change this sorting, it is possible to sort the results in a certain way. growing(ascendingor decreasing(descending) by any field available in the dataset.

Sorting results
JSON
{
    "Datasets": "emails.order(EmailTotalPassages=ascending)",
    "q": "doc{12345678900}"
}
4️⃣ Paginate results

Some datasets return extensive lists of recordsWhen more results are available, the return will include the field. Nextcontaining the Next page identifier.

To retrieve the next records, use the parameter. .next(x) in the field Datasets, right after the dataset name, indicating the value returned in Next and maintaining the same parameter q from the original query.



Retrieve next page
JSON
{
    "Datasets": "media_profile_and_exposure.next(AoJw14Wy0ZYDP0No...)",
    "q": "doc{12345678900}"
}
📘
Important:

Pagination does not incur any additional cost, and subsequent pages remain available for up to 3 hours after the initial search.

5️⃣ Limit the number of results

Some datasets may return a large number of records for the same entity, such as Awards and Certifications, Contacts, or Relationships.

In these scenarios, it may be beneficial to define a maximum limit on the number of results returned, ensuring faster, more organized responses that are aligned with your business workflow.

To do this, use the parameter. .limit(x) in the field Datasets, right after the name of the dataset to which you want to apply this customization.



Limiting the number of results
JSON
{
    "Datasets": "awards_and_certifications.limit(10)",
    "q": "doc{12345678900}"
}
6️⃣ Use reference dates

It is possible to conduct queries considering a specific date as a reference, returning a snapshot of the data at that moment. Sending the keys is mandatory for this. referencedate{DATA} and dateformat{FORMATO} within the parameter q.

This feature is useful for historical analysis and backtesting, without requiring any additional request to the BigDataCorp team.

Examples of calls with a reference date.
JSON
{
    "Datasets": "addresses",
    "q": "doc{12345678900},referencedate{03-09-2018},dateformat{dd-MM-yyyy}"
}


JSON
{
    "Datasets": "political_involvement",
    "q": "doc{12345678900},referencedate{12-2020},dateformat{MM-yyyy}"
}


🚧
If the date submitted is invalid, the current date will be considered as the reference.
7️⃣ Practical Summary
Field selection → defines which information will be returned.
Filters → define which records should appear in the return.
Sorting → defines the order in which the data is presented.
Pagination → allows you to navigate through large volumes of records.
Limitation → defines the maximum number of records that should appear in the return.
Reference date → returns a historical snapshot of the data.

These resources can be combined to create leaner, more organized responses that are aligned with your workflow.

Next steps

Now that you know how to customize query results, proceed to:

Additional resources – use of dochash, tags, and complementary functionalities.
✨ From here, you already know how to shape the response from your queries. Now you can explore the additional features!



Updated about 2 months ago

Request Parameters

4️⃣ Paginate results
5️⃣ Limit the number of results
6️⃣ Use reference dates

Online Ads
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset gathers information about advertisements published by the individual in question on online peer-to-peer buying and selling portals and marketplaces. The results include advertiser profiles inferred from public information present in the advertisements; more than one profile may be associated with the same person, depending on the different portals and contexts identified. The data only represents the individual's activity as an informal seller and does not include marketplaces aimed at formal sellers.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Digital Presence documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: online_ads.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
activeads	Number of active ads	Bigger or equal	Any integer
totalads	Total ads posted	Bigger or equal	Any integer
admaxvalue	Maximum value among the ads	Bigger or equal	Any decimal number
adminvalue	Minimum value between ads	Bigger or equal	Any decimal number
portal	The website where the ads were published.	Equal	Any portal name
category	Category of published ads	Equal	Any category name
Limit

This dataset allows you to limit the number of records returned using the modifier .limit(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Electoral Candidates
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset gathers information on individuals' participation in current and historical elections, including general indicators of candidacy and election, as well as details of each electoral process, with registration data declared to the Electoral Court and campaign finance reports, covering income, donations, and associated expenses.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Political Engagement documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: election_candidate_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month
BODY PARAMS
q
string
required

A string that defines the query's input parameters, used to specify the identifiers and filters applicable to the dataset.

Datasets
string
required

String containing the technical names of the datasets to be queried.

Limit
number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

RESPONSE
200

Dataset return parameters.

RESPONSE BODY
object
Result
array of objects
required

Result lists

object
ElectionCandidateData
object
required

The main object returned by the dataset, containing consolidated information related to the person consulted.

ELECTIONCANDIDATEDATA OBJECT
QueryId
string
required

Unique caller ID.

ElapsedMilliseconds
number
required

The number of milliseconds elapsed during the call.

QueryDate
string

Class Councils
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset gathers information about the respondent's participation in professional councils, professional associations, and professional bodies. The results include aggregated indicators, such as the total number of identified affiliations, as well as details of each associated professional registration, allowing for analyses of performance, regularity, and history within professional entities.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Professionals documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: class_organization.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
organization name	Name of the professional entity or professional council.	Equal	CREFITO, CREA, CRM, among others
organizationtype	Organization type	Equal	CLASS ASSOCIATION, PROFESSIONAL ORGANIZATION
organizationchapter	Federal unit of registration	Equal	RJ, SP, MG, AM, PE, SC, ES, among others
status	Professional registration status	Equal	ACTIVE, DEACTIVATED, TRANSFERRED, CANCELLED, INTERRUPTED, REQUEST
category	Professional category or qualification associated with the registration.	Equal	Physiotherapist, Civil Engineer, Agronomist, Technologist, among others.

To understand how to apply filters, sorting, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month

string
required

number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

200

POST
Configurable Recency Registration Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns the basic registration data of the person being consulted, such as name, CPF (Brazilian tax identification number), date of birth, and parentage, with the possibility of configuring the maximum recency of the data from the Federal Revenue Service. If the available information does not meet the defined recency criterion, the dataset automatically performs a real-time re-evaluation of the official sources, allowing it to meet more stringent business rules and high data update requirements.

For documents with the status "DECEASED OWNER", no dynamic updates are performed, regardless of the configured recency, since no future changes are expected in these records.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Basic Data documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: basic_data_with_configurable_recency.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.090
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.085
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.081
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.077
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.073
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 0.069
5.000.001 - 10.000.000	BRL 0.066
10.000.001 - 50.000.000	BRL 0.063
50,000,001 and above	BRL 0.060
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing you to control the recency of the data and perform similarity validations between the information provided in the input and the data returned.

Key	Description	Possible Values
max_days_since_update	Defines the maximum time, in days, since the last accepted update for the data.	Whole number (minimum 7, default 30)
name	Allows you to compare the entered name with the name found in the database.	Free text
mothername	Allows you to compare the mother's name entered with the one found in the database.	Free text
fathername	It allows you to compare the father's name entered with the one found in the database.	Free text

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

Family Lawsuit Distribution Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset provides aggregated data on legal proceedings involving first-degree relatives of the person being consulted. The result consolidates distribution indicators based on the main fields of the Legal Proceedings dataset, allowing for a summary view of the family's legal history for quick analysis, without the need to individually evaluate each identified proceeding. For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Legal Proceedings documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: family_lawsuits_distribution_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.100
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.096
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.092
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.088
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.084
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	R$68,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
capturedate	Process capture date	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
closedate	Process closing date	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
cnjsubject	Case number or subject name in the CNJ (National Council of Justice)	Equal	Number or text
cnjproceduretype	Case number or type name in the CNJ (National Council of Justice).	Equal	Number or text
courtlevel	Court level	Equal	1, 2, 3
courtname	Court name	Equal	List of courts
courttype	Court type	Equal	Civil, Criminal, Tax, Social Security, Labor, Electoral, among others.
partypolarity	Polarity of the party in the process	Equal	ACTIVE, PASSIVE, NEUTRAL
partytype	Type of participation of the entity in the process	Equal	AUTHOR, DEFENDANT, LAWYER, JUDGE, INMATE, among others
noticed	Process notification date	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
state	Status of the process	Equal	UF
status	Process status	Contains	ACTIVE, ARCHIVED, CLOSED, UNDER APPEAL, FINAL JUDGMENT, among others
resjudicatadate	Date of final judgment	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
value	Value discussed in the process	Between	[minimum, maximum]

To understand how to apply filters, sorting, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS

POST
Lawsuits Distribution Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset consolidates aggregated information about legal proceedings in which the consulted entity is involved, allowing for a quick overview of the distribution of these proceedings across different dimensions, such as type, court, state, status, and party role. The return is based on the main fields of the Legal Proceedings dataset, making it suitable for exploratory analyses and preliminary assessments, without the need to consume large volumes of detailed data.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Legal Proceedings documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: lawsuits_distribution_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing you to refine the analysis and change how the aggregated data is returned.

Key	Description	Possible Values
alternativedistributionparameter	Defines an alternative field for generating additional aggregations in the returned dataset.	PartyType

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
capturedate	Process capture date	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
closedate	Process closing date	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
cnjsubject	Case number or subject name in the CNJ (National Council of Justice)	Equal	Number or text
cnjproceduretype	Case number or type name in the CNJ (National Council of Justice).	Equal	Number or text
courtlevel	Court level	Equal	1, 2, 3
courtname	Court name	Equal	List of courts
courttype	Court type	Equal	Civil, Criminal, Tax, Social Security, Labor, Electoral, among others.
partypolarity	Polarity of the party in the process	Equal	ACTIVE, PASSIVE, NEUTRAL
partytype	Type of participation of the entity in the process	Equal	AUTHOR, DEFENDANT, LAWYER, JUDGE, INMATE, among others
noticed	Process notification date	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
state	Status of the process	Equal	UF
status	Process status	Contains	ACTIVE, ARCHIVED, CLOSED, UNDER APPEAL, FINAL JUDGMENT, among others
resjudicatadate	Date of final judgment	Between	[yyyy-MM-dd, yyyy-MM-dd]
value	Value discussed in the process	Between	[minimum, maximum]

To understand how to apply filters, sorting, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Return with optional key alternativedistributionparameter

When the optional key alternativedistributionparameter If the value is specified, the dataset includes an additional block of aggregations in the return, calculated from the values ​​found for the indicated field (for example, PartyTypeThis additional return is available within the object. LawsuitsDistributionByAlternativeParameterValues, which relates each identified value of the alternative parameter (e.g., APPLICANT, RESPONDENT, PLAINTIFF) to its respective distributions for the same aggregated dimensions of the main return (such as process type, court, status, state, and other distributions).

Popularity Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset consolidates information related to individuals' public exposure in political, judicial, economic, social, and media contexts, allowing for analysis of the degree of influence exerted on society. The output includes a normalized influence score, which reflects the individual's level of public exposure, considering the relevance and scope of the different associated signals.

For interpretation purposes, the higher the influence score, the greater the individual's public visibility and the potential reach of their actions or positions tend to be. The score composition takes into account, among other factors, political participation, and is adjusted according to the type and scope of the office being contested.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Public Exposure documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: influence_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.100
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.096
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.092
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.088
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.084
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	R$68,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Registration Data

https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns registration data for the person being searched, gathering information commonly used in registration and profile enrichment processes, such as identification data, emails, addresses, and phone numbers associated with the individual.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Contacts documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: registration_data.

Website Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset gathers information about websites associated with the consulted entity, considering recurring captures made over the last 24 months to compose a historical overview. The return includes aggregated data on the number of websites identified, the diversity of domains associated with the entity, and the association of different entities with the same domain. It is important to note that the dataset returns websites, not domains: a domain can contain multiple websites, just as a website can be distributed across more than one domain.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Digital Presence documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: domains_extended.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
hasssl	Indicates whether the website uses SSL.	Equal	true, false
isactive	Indicates whether the site is active.	Equal	true, false
socialnetworks	Social networks associated with the website	Contains	facebook, twitter, linkedin, etc.
ispname	Hosting Provider	Equal	Provider name, such as AMAZON
paymentmethods	Identified payment methods	Contains	credit card, invoice, etc.
paymentservices	Payment services used	Contains	paypal, pagseguro, etc.
framework	Framework or platform used on the website	Equal	Platform name
visitor range	Estimated range of monthly visitors.	Equal	Access range per month
domainclass	Domain class	Equal	PORTAL, BLOG, ECOMMERCE, CORPORATE, GOVERNMENT, NEWS, EDUCATIONAL
IsRegisteredInWhoIsData	Indicates whether the site is registered in the name of the entity	Equal	true, false
Limit

This dataset allows you to limit the number of records returned using the modifier .limit(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS

Limit

Professional Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns aggregated and historical information about the professional activity of the person consulted. The return consolidates current and past work relationships, including records of formal employment and indications of informal professional activity, such as self-employment or service provision.

The data is suitable for analyzing professional profiles, employment history, and evaluating a person's career path.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Professionals documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: occupation_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
sector	Employer's sector	Equal	PRIVATE, PUBLIC
companyname	Company or entity name	Contains	Full or partial name
area	Area of ​​operation within the company	Contains	Marketing, Finance, Technology, among others.
level	Job title, function or position held	Contains	Any job description
status	Employment status	Equal	ACTIVE, INACTIVE

To understand how to apply filters, sorting, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.

Government Debtors
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset provides information on taxpayers registered as primary debtors in outstanding debts to the Federal Government and the FGTS (Brazilian Severance Indemnity Fund), according to the most recent records made available by the PGFN (Attorney General's Office for the National Treasury). Because this is not an on-demand database, the data may be outdated, including debts that have already been paid or not reflecting recent additions.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Economic and Financial documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: government_debtors.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Limit

Electoral Donors
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset compiles information on electoral donations made and received by the consulted entity, including aggregated attributes of quantity and total value, as well as details of each donation, such as the politician involved, value, date, and indication of whether it was made digitally. For interpretation purposes, the data includes financial flows in both directions, allowing the identification of both donations made and received by the entity. When a reference date is provided in the query, the electoral cycles considered are counted retroactively from that date.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Political Engagement documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: electoral_donors.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Emails from Related People
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

A dataset that returns emails associated with people related to the queried individual, with classification and validation attributes that support contact analysis, segmentation, and automated decision-making.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Contacts documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: related_people_emails.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters

The filters below allow you to refine the results returned by this dataset according to specific criteria.

Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
relationshiptype	Type of relationship	Equal	coworker, neighbor, brother, nephew, mother, son, household, grandson, spouse, relative, grandparent, uncle, cousin, father, partner, related
type	Email type	Contains	corporate, personal
isactive	If email is active	Equal	true, false
isrecent	If the email is recent	Equal	true, false
ismain	If email is primary	Equal	true, false
validationstatus	validation status	Equal	VALID, ACCEPT_ALL, UNKNOWN, POSSIBLE_SPAM_TRAP, INVALID, HARDBOUNCE, RISKY, ACCEPT
domain	Email domain	Equal	gmail.com, yahoo.com.br, ...

To understand how to apply filters, sorting, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month
BODY PARAMS
q
string
required

A string that defines the query's input parameters, used to specify the identifiers and filters applicable to the dataset.

Datasets
string
required

String containing the technical names of the datasets to be queried.

Limit
number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

RESPONSE
200

Dataset return parameters.

RESPONSE BODY
object
Result
array of objects
required

Result lists

object
RelatedPeopleEmails
array of objects
required
object
RelationshipType
string
required

Indicates the type of relationship associated with this record. (coworker, neighbor, brother, nephew, mother, son, household, grandson, spouse, relative, grandparent, uncle, cousin, father, partner, related)

EmailAddress
string
required

Email address.

Domain
string
required

Email address domain (after the '@' symbol).

UserName
string
required

Username (before the '@' symbol).

Type
string
required

Composite field that describes the related entity and the email context. (RELATED - {DOCUMENT-NUMBER} - {RELATIONSHIP-TYPE} - {EMAIL-TYPE (CORPORATE or PERSONAL)})

EmailTotalPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email has been viewed for this entity.

EmailBadPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email was seen for this entity in a suspicious situation.

EmailCrawlingPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email was seen for this entity by a crawling or capturing process.

EmailValidationPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email has been seen for this entity through a validation process.

EmailQueryPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email was seen by this entity in a query by query.

EmailMonthAveragePassages
integer
required

Average monthly number of times this email was viewed by this entity.

EmailNumberOfEntities
integer
required

Number of different entities associated with this email.

Last3MonthsPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email has been viewed by this entity in the last 3 months.

Last6MonthsPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email has been viewed by this entity in the last 6 months.

Last12MonthsPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email has been viewed by this entity in the last 12 months.

Last18MonthsPassages
integer
required

Number of times this email has been viewed by this entity in the last 18 months.

Priority
integer
required

Priority level of this email for this entity.

IsMain
boolean
required

Indicates whether this is the entity's primary email address.

IsRecent
boolean
required

Indicates whether this email is recent for this entity.

IsActive
boolean
required

Indicates whether this email is active for this entity.

ValidationStatus
string
required

The last recorded validation status of the email validation process. (VALID, ACCEPT_ALL, UNKNOWN, POSSIBLE_SPAM_TRAP, INVALID, HARDBOUNCE, RISKY, ACCEPT)

LastValidationDate
string
required

Date the email was last validated.

FirstPassageDate
string
required

Date this email was first seen for this entity.

LastPassageDate
string
required

Date this email was last seen for this entity.

CreationDate
string
required

Date the record was created.

LastUpdateDate
string
required

Date the record was last updated.

QueryId
string
required

Unique caller ID.

ElapsedMilliseconds
number
required

The number of milliseconds elapsed during the call.

QueryDate
string
required

The date when the request was received and processed.

Status
object
required

Status list for each dataset.

VIEW ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES
Evidences
object
required

List of evidence used for each dataset.

Companies
POST
Addresses of Related People
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns addresses associated with people related to the individual being consulted, allowing for the analysis of indirect links, address characteristics, recency, priority, and nature of the association. The data supports relationship analysis, segmentation, and automated decision-making in flows that require enhanced contact context.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Contacts documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: related_people_addresses.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters

The filters below allow you to refine the results returned by this dataset based on relationship type, address characteristics, status, recency, priority, and location.

Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
relationshiptype	Type of relationship	Equal	coworker, neighbor, brother, nephew, mother, son, household, grandson, spouse, relative, grandparent, uncle, cousin, father, partner, related
type	Type of address	Contains	work, home
isratified	If it is ratified	Equal	true, false
isactive	If it is active	Equal	true, false
isrecent	if it's recent	Equal	true, false
ismain	If it is main	Equal	true, false
state	State acronym	Equal	RJ, SP, MG, ES...
zipcode	zip code	Starts with	Any full zip code or first x digits
Limit

This dataset allows you to limit the number of records returned using the modifier .limit(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month
BODY PARAMS
q
string
required

A string that defines the query's input parameters, used to specify the identifiers and filters applicable to the dataset.

Datasets
string
required

String containing the technical names of the datasets to be queried.

Limit
number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

RESPONSE
200

Dataset return parameters.

RESPONSE BODY
object
Result
array of objects
required

Result lists

object
RelatedPeopleAddresses
array of objects
required
object
RelationshipType
string
required

Indicates the type of relationship associated with this record. (coworker, neighbor, brother, nephew, mother, son, household, grandson, spouse, relative, grandparent, uncle, cousin, father, partner, related)

Typology
string
required

Type of street (Street, Avenue, etc.).

Title
string
required

Ownership of the address (ie Praça, Nossa Senhora, Barão).

AddressMain
string
required

Address core.

Number
string
required

Address port number.

Complement
string
required

Address complement (ie Block, Apartment, etc...).

Neighborhood
string
required

Address neighborhood.

ZipCode
string
required

Postal code of the address.

City
string
required

Address city.

State
string
required

State abbreviation for the address (e.g., SP, RJ, etc.).

Country
string
required

Address country.

Type
string
required

Composite field that describes the related entity and the address context. (RELATED - {DOCUMENT-NUMBER} - {RELATIONSHIP-TYPE} - {ADDRESS-TYPE (HOME, WORK or OFFICIAL REGISTRATION)}).

ComplementType
string
required

Address type calculated from the complement of the address (APARTMENT, HOUSE, STORE, COMMERCIAL OFFICE).

HouseholdCode
string
required

A code that uniquely identifies each individual address - each apartment or room within a building, each house within a village, etc.

BuildingCode
string
required

Alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies a building or construction, that is, it works with the address up to the level of its door number.

HouseholdTotalPassages
integer
required

Number of times this Household code has been seen for this entity.

HouseholdBadPassages
integer
required

Number of times this household code was seen for this entity in a suspicious situation.

HouseholdCrawlingPassages
integer
required

Number of Household code passes with the entity originating a capture.

HouseholdValidationPassages
integer
required

Number of passes of the Household code with the entity originating a validation.

HouseholdQueryPassages
integer
required

Number of Household code passes with the entity originating a query.

HouseholdMonthAveragePassages
integer
required

Average number of times the Household code was seen with the entity per month.

HouseholdNumberOfEntities
integer
required

Number of distinct entities associated with this Household code.

BuildingTotalPassages
integer
required

Number of times this Building code was seen for this entity.

BuildingBadPassages
integer
required

Number of times this Building code was seen for this entity in a suspicious situation.

BuildingCrawlingPassages
integer
required

Number of passes of the building code (Building code) with the entity that originates a capture.

BuildingValidationPassages
integer
required

Number of building code passes (Building code) with the entity that originates from a validation.

BuildingQueryPassages
integer
required

Number of passages of the building code (Building code) with the entity that originates a query.

BuildingMonthAveragePassages
integer
required

Average number of times the Building code was viewed with the entity per month.

BuildingNumberOfEntities
integer
required

Number of distinct entities associated with this building code (Building code).

BuildingNumberOfHouseholds
integer
required

Number of distinct Households associated with this building (Building code).

Last3MonthsPassages
integer
required

Total number of passages in the last 3 months.

Last6MonthsPassages
integer
required

Total number of passages in the last 6 months.

Last12MonthsPassages
integer
required

Total number of passages in the last 12 months.

Last18MonthsPassages
integer
required

Total number of passages in the last 18 months.

Priority
integer
required

Indicates the address priority (if there is more than one).

IsMain
boolean
required

Indicates whether the address is the primary.

IsRecent
boolean
required

Indicates whether this address is recent.

IsActive
boolean
required

Indicates whether this address is active.

IsRatified
boolean
required

Indicates whether the address has been ratified by the postal reference base (DNE or similar).

FirstPassageDate
string
required

Date of this household's first pass with this entity.

LastPassageDate
string
required

Date of last pass of this household with this entity.

CreationDate
string
required

Record creation date

LastUpdateDate
string
required

Date of last record update.

HasOptIn
boolean
required

Flag that indicates whether there has been explicit acceptance of orders and advertisements by this address in our database

AdditionalOutputData
object
required

List of additional data referring to the address, such as IBGE Code.

HAS ADDITIONAL FIELDS
Latitude
number
required

Address latitude.

Longitude
number
required

Address length.

QueryId
string
required

Unique caller ID.

ElapsedMilliseconds
number
required

The number of milliseconds elapsed during the call.

QueryDate
string
required

The date when the request was received and processed.

Status
object
required

Status list for each dataset.

VIEW ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES
Evidences
object
required

List of evidence used for each dataset.

Adresses
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

A dataset that returns addresses associated with the queried individual, including classification, recency, priority, and validation attributes, as well as aggregated information that facilitates contact analysis, segmentation, and automated decision-making.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Contacts documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: addresses_extended.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing you to improve query quality, control address deduplication, and enable comparison mechanisms between entered addresses and those found in the databases.

Key	Description	Possible Values
zipcode	Postal code of the consulted address. Can be used in conjunction with addressnumber to improve the accuracy of property identification.	Address zip code
addressnumber	Number of the address consulted. Can be used in conjunction with zipcode to improve the accuracy of property identification.	Address number
address	Address searched (street and additional information, without house number or postal code). Can be used with withmatchrate{true} for comparison with addresses associated with the individual.	Address typology and core
returnonlydifferentaddresses	It only returns addresses that are different from those provided.zipcode and addressnumber), provided they are associated with the same account holder.	true, false (Default: false)
withmatchrate	Indicates whether the similarity between the given address should be calculated (zipcode + addressnumber + address) and the returned addresses. The result is displayed in the field. MatchRate, where 100 indicates an exact match.	true, false (Default: false)

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

Filters

The filters below allow you to refine the results returned by this dataset according to address characteristics, such as type, status, recency, priority, location, and nature of the association.

Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
Type	Type of address	Equal	work, home
isratified	If it is ratified	Equal	true, false
isactive	If it is active	Equal	true, false
isrecent	if it's recent	Equal	true, false
ismain	If it is main	Equal	true, false
isindirect	If the association is indirect	Equal	true, false
state	State acronym	Equal	RJ, SP, MG, ES, ...
zipcode	zip code	Starts with	Any full zip code or first x digits
Limit

This dataset allows you to limit the number of records returned using the modifier .limit(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

Media Profile and Exposure
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset gathers information about individuals' public exposure in open media sources, including associated journalistic content, aggregated visibility indicators, and the nature of the exposure, as well as sentiment analysis of the identified mentions. The returned news items are associated with the queried entity through inference based on the name identified in the content and, when provided, the keywords submitted in the query. Because the publications do not contain formal identifiers (such as CPF numbers), very common or short names may result in less precise associations or content unrelated to the queried individual.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Public Exposure documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: media_profile_and_exposure.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.090
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.085
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.081
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.077
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.073
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 61,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing you to refine the query or change how the data is analyzed and returned.

Key	Description	Possible Values
keywords	Allows you to restrict the returned content based on specific keywords, separated by commas.	Any text.
Ex.: keywords[lavagem de dinheiro, vice-prefeito, corrupção]
keywords_operator	Define the logic for combining the given keywords.	OR, AND

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
title	News title	Contains	Any text
sourcename	Name of the source responsible for the news	Contains	Any text
categories	News category	Contains	Any text
label	Sentiment rating assigned to the news story.	Equal	NEGATIVE, POSITIVE, NEUTRAL, UNDEFINED, POLARIZED, SLIGHTLY_POSITIVE, SLIGHTLY_NEGATIVE
Limits and Pagination

This dataset allows you to limit the number of records returned using the modifier .limit(x) in the field Datasets And, when more records are available, it is possible to paginate the results using the modifier. .next(x).

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Exposure indicators

The indicators returned summarize different dimensions of the entity's media presence:

MediaExposureLevel: indicates the level of exposure based on the total volume of news associated with the entity, considering, by default, the content identified in the last 6 months. The indicator reflects the intensity of the entity's media presence during the analyzed period.
CelebrityLevel: reflects the average monthly frequency with which the entity appears in journalistic content, calculated, by default, from mentions identified in the last 6 months. This indicator seeks to capture the recurrence of the entity's public visibility.
UnpopularityLevel: estimates the degree of unpopularity of the entity through a weighted average that considers the volume of negative news, the proportion of mentions with unfavorable sentiment, and the relevance of the sources in which the news was published. The calculation uses, by default, content from the last 6 months and distributes the result on a categorical scale of unpopularity.
Search by name

When the search is performed exclusively by name (key) name{XXXX} or name{XXXX}, shortname{XX}In cases where no other keys are sent or other datasets are included, the search is conducted without using an identity document (CPF). In these cases, the search independently considers:

the full name provided (name)
the shortname, when informed, or the first and last name derived from name

If the first and last name (shortnameEven if names are considered to have low uniqueness, the search is performed using only the full name, in order to avoid an excess of results for homonyms.

If other keys are sent in the query or other datasets are included in the request, full dataset processing will be applied.

Interpreting the field SentimentAnalysis

The field SentimentAnalysis It indicates the predominant sentiment identified in each news item associated with the consulted entity, based on text analysis models applied to journalistic content.

Label	Description
NEGATIVE	Predominance of negative sentiment
POSITIVE	Predominance of positive sentiment
NEUTRAL	Absence of a clear predominance of feeling.
UNDEFINED	It was not possible to infer sentiment.
POLARIZED	Simultaneous presence of positive and negative feelings.
SLIGHTLY_POSITIVE	Positive trend with neutrality
SLIGHTLY_NEGATIVE	Negative trend with neutrality
RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.

Sports Exposure
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset identifies direct and indirect links between the respondent and institutions within the sports environment, including clubs, federations, confederations, and other entities related to sports activities. The data provided allows for the analysis of institutional relationships, roles played, level of closeness of the link, and activity status, and may also include indirect associations through family or corporate relationships, when available in the sources.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Professionals documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: sports_exposure.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.070
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.066
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.063
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.060
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.057
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 46,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
relationshiplevel	Level of relationship between the individual and the sports organization.	Equal	DIRECT, INDIRECT 1ST LEVEL, INDIRECT 2ND LEVEL, INDIRECT 3RD LEVEL, INDIRECT 4TH LEVEL, BUSINESS PARTNER
roll	Function performed by the individual in the sports organization.	Equal	ASSISTANT COACH, COACH, COUNCIL MEMBER, DIRECTOR, FORMER PRESIDENT, MANAGER, PLAYER, PRESIDENT, REFEREE, STAFF, VICE PRESIDENT
isactive	Allows you to filter active or inactive sports affiliations.	Equal	true, false

To understand how to apply filters, sorting, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Interpreting the field RelationshipLevel

The field RelationshipLevel This indicates whether the identified sporting involvement is direct (by the person being consulted) or indirect (through a relationship with another person). In indirect cases, the level reflects the degree of kinship or corporate affiliation, as per the table below.

RelationshipLevel	Relationship Types	Relationship Description
DIRECT	—	The sports affiliation of the person consulted.
INDIRECT 1ST LEVEL	MOTHER, FATHER, SON, SPOUSE	First-degree kinship
INDIRECT 2ND LEVEL	BROTHER, GRANDSON, GRANDPARENT	Second-degree kinship
INDIRECT 3RD LEVEL	UNCLE, NEPHEW	Third-degree kinship
INDIRECT 4TH LEVEL	COUSIN	Fourth-level kinship
BUSINESS PARTNER	PARTNER	Corporate relationship
Interpreting the field Role

The field Role It describes the role performed by the individual within the sports organization, encompassing executive, administrative, technical, sporting, and operational positions, according to the nomenclature used by the institutions themselves.

Office	Description
ASSISTANT COACH	Assistant or analyst participating in the technical committee
COACH	Trainer
COUNCIL MEMBER	Member of the administrative board
DIRECTOR	Director of one of the sectors that make up the club.
FORMER PRESIDENT	Former president of the deliberative council
MANAGER	Coordinator, executive, manager, or department head.
PLAYER	Professional player
PRESIDENT	General president of the club or of the deliberative council
REFEREE	Referee
STAFF	Employees in general
VICE PRESIDENT	Vice President at any level
Interpreting the field IsActive

The field IsActive This indicates whether the sports affiliation is active at the time of the query. When sources do not provide explicit start or end dates for the affiliation, the status is inferred based on the date of the last record capture: affiliations updated within the last 30 days are considered active; otherwise, they are considered inactive.

Historical Basic Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns the history of changes to an individual's registration information with the Brazilian Federal Revenue Service, including name changes and CPF (Brazilian taxpayer ID) status over time. This information allows for temporal analyses applicable to credit models, background check processes, and registration validations in onboarding journeys. The history only considers initial registration information and changes that occurred after reaching the age of majority (18 years), not returning data for CPF numbers of minors or documents not covered by the internal database.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Basic Data documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: historical_basic_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.030
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.028
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.027
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.026
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.025
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 21,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing you to validate information entered in the input and compare historical data with records from the Federal Revenue Service.

Key	Description	Possible Values
name	It allows for the comparison of the entered name with the name found in the database, returning the percentage of similarity.	Free text
mothername	It allows for the comparison of the mother's name provided with the name found in the database, returning the percentage of similarity.	Free text
birthdate	It allows you to compare the entered date of birth with the date found in the database.	Valid date
dateformat	Defines the date format specified in the birthdate parameter.	formatting string

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

School and Academic History
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

A dataset that returns consolidated information about the educational and academic history of the person consulted, including indicators of educational level, affiliation with higher education, and records associated with academic training.

The return combines declared data, public records, and information inferred from multiple sources and statistical correlations. In cases of inferred information, the record detail explicitly indicates this condition in the institution field, ensuring transparency in data interpretation.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Professionals documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: university_student_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
level	Level of academic training	Equal	UNDERGRADUATE, GRADUATE, POST-GRADUATE, among others
institution	Name of the educational institution	Contains	Full or partial name
specialization area	Area of ​​specialization or training	Contains	Engineering, Medicine, Economics, Law, among others.
Limit

This dataset allows you to limit the number of records returned using the modifier .limit(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Family Political History
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset generates a timeline with information about the political participation of family members of the individual being consulted, including aggregations that allow for the identification of involvement in different regions, elected positions, and political parties. The data enables the analysis of the level of political influence and exposure of the family as a whole.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Political Engagement documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: family_political_history.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.070
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.066
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.063
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.060
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.057
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 46,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Financial Information of Family Members
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns aggregated financial information about the surveyed individual's family unit, allowing for the assessment of income composition, primary income source, and estimates of household spending and disposable income. For classification purposes, an individual is considered the head of household when they contribute 70% or more of the total income, while financial dependents are those contributing less than 10%, including minors and individuals in situations of economic dependency. The data provided supports economic analyses, risk modeling, and financial capacity assessments.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Economic and Financial documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: family_financial_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.090
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.085
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.081
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.077
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.073
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 61,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Financial Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns consolidated financial information for the individual being consulted, combining statistical inferences and public data to provide a comprehensive view of income, assets, and tax return history. The return includes different income estimates calculated using distinct methodologies, as well as an estimate of the individual's total assets.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Economic and Financial documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: financial_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Ranges of returned values

Income estimates are presented in ranges of minimum wages (MW), while the net worth estimate ( TotalAssets ) is presented in ranges of value in Brazilian Reais (R$) .

Field	Possible Values
IncomeEstimates	NO INFORMATION, 1/2 TO 1 SM, FROM 1 TO 1.5 SM, UP TO 2 SM, 2 TO 4 SM, 4 TO 10 SM, 10 TO 20 SM, ABOVE 20 SM
IncomeEstimates - BIGDATA_V2	NO INFORMATION, 0 TO 1 SM, 1 TO 2 SM, 2 TO 3 SM, 3 TO 5 SM, 5 TO 7 SM, 7 TO 10 SM, 10 TO 15 SM, 15 TO 20 SM, OVER 20 SM
TotalAssets	BELOW 100K, 100K TO 250K, 250K TO 500K, 500K TO 1MM, 1 TO 5MM, ABOVE 5MM

Socio-Demographic Information
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns socio-demographic estimates attributed to an individual based on public statistical research and population surveys. The information is inferred from geographic location, population profile, and aggregate characteristics of the region associated with the individual, representing statistical approximations and not individual statements.

The returned data is organized into independent demographic records, reflecting specific segments of surveys or statistical databases, and may vary according to origin, level of aggregation, and geographic scope.

For more information on business context, applications, and usage criteria, please refer to the Socio-Demographic documentation.

Technical name of the dataset:* demographic_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters

The filters allow you to restrict the returned records according to the search origin, the level of statistical aggregation, and the geographic scope of the data.

Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Values
dataorigin	Origin of the research or statistical survey	Equal	IBGE, MTE
dataaggregationlevel	Level of aggregation of statistical data	Equal	---
datacountry	Reference country for the research	Equal	Brazil, Argentina, Mexico, etc.

To understand how to apply filters, sorting, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Ranges of returned values

The estimates returned by this dataset are presented in discrete ranges or categories, as defined by the source statistical surveys.

Field	Possible Values
EstimatedIncomeRange	1 - UP TO 2 SM, 2 - 2 TO 4 SM, 3 - 4 TO 10 SM, 4 - 10 TO 20 SM, 5 - OVER 20 SM
EstimatedInstructionLevel	01 - Illiterate, 02 - Up to 5th Grade Incomplete, 03 - 5th Grade Completed Elementary School, 04 - 6th to 9th Grade Elementary School, 05 - Completed Elementary School, 06 - Incomplete High School, 07 - Completed High School, 08 - Incomplete Higher Education, 09 - Completed Higher Education, 10 - Master's Degree, 11 - Doctorate, 00 - Daycare, Preschool, Literacy Class, Former Primary School, Former Middle School, Elementary School, High School, Higher Education, Specialization
SocialClass	A, B, C, D, E
RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history

Licenses and Authorizations
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns information about licenses and authorizations associated with the consulted individual, generally linked to the exercise of regulated economic activities, especially in the context of self-employment. The return includes records issued by official bodies, when available, and may not contain data for CPF numbers (Brazilian individual taxpayer registration numbers) that do not have regulatory requirements associated with their professional activity.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Professionals documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: licenses_and_authorizations.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
licensetype	Type of license or authorization	Equal	ANTT, CADASTUR

To understand how to apply filters, sorting, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Interpreting the field TypeDescription

The field TypeDescription This describes the scope of the license or authorization identified in the return, indicating the type of regulated activity to which the record is associated. Some examples of possible values ​​include:

ANTT – Authorization to operate in road freight transport activities nationwide.
CADASTUR – Authorization to operate in activities related to the tourism sector, nationwide.

Level of Political Involvement
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset consolidates information related to the political involvement of the respondent and returns a normalized total score, allowing for the relative classification of the level of political involvement on a single scale. The score calculation considers factors such as donations to electoral campaigns, direct participation in previous elections, and current holding of public office.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Political Engagement documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: political_involvement.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
