Web Passages
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns aggregated information about an individual's web browsing history, considering frequency, diversity of contexts, and temporal attributes associated with the identifications made in BigDataCorp's processes. The data provided supports analyses of digital presence, behavior, and activity over time.

For more information on context, concepts, and applications related to digital behavior, please refer to the Behavior category documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: passages.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the accepted complementary keys that directly impact how the aggregate metrics for this dataset are calculated.

Key	Description	Possible Values
minpassage threshold	Establishes a threshold with the passed value that determines the minimum number of identical associations a document must have to be counted as a pass in the aggregation fields. NumberOfEmails, NumberOfPhones, NumberOfAddresses, NumberOfIpAddresses and NumberOfDevices.	Integer

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month

Awards and Certifications
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset gathers aggregated and detailed information about awards received and certifications obtained by the person consulted. The return includes different types of recognition, including institutional, academic, or private awards, as well as professional, technical, or compliance certifications, when identified in public sources. The data is organized into separate lists for awards and certifications, allowing a structured view of the recognitions associated with the entity.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Professionals documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: awards_and_certifications.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.090
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.085
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.081
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.077
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.073
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 61,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
award name	prize name	Contains	Any name or part of the name of the award.
awardingorganizationname	Name of the organization that awarded the prize.	Equal	Any entity name
certification name	certification name	Contains	Any name or part of the name of the certification (e.g., PMP, ISO-27001)
certifying entity	Name of the certifying entity	Equal	Any certification body (e.g., ISO, PMI)
certification status	Certification status	Equal	ACTIVE, INACTIVE
Limit

This dataset allows you to limit the number of records returned using the modifier .limit(x) in the field DatasetsThis limit is applied independently to lists Awards and Certificates.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history

Make a request to see history.

q
string
required

A string that defines the query's input parameters, used to specify the identifiers and filters applicable to the dataset.

Datasets
string
required

String containing the technical names of the datasets to be queried.

Limit
number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

RESPONSE
200

Dataset return parameters.

RESPONSE BODY
object
Result
array of objects
required

Result lists

object
Awards and Certifications
object
required

The main object returned by the dataset, containing consolidated information related to the person consulted.

AWARDSANDCERTIFICATIONS OBJECT
QueryId
string
required

Unique caller ID.

ElapsedMilliseconds
number
required

The number of milliseconds elapsed during the call.

QueryDate
string
required

The date when the request was received and processed.

Status
object
required

Status list for each dataset.

VIEW ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES

object
required

List of evidence used for each dataset.

Companies
POST
Presence in Collection
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset indicates an individual's presence in or involvement with debt collection processes, based on the identification of instances of contact, collection attempts, or exposure to credit recovery mechanisms. The return does not provide information on the debt amount, the creditor, or the nature of the obligation, only the existence of debt collection within a specific period.

In addition to the total number of occurrences, the dataset provides consolidated temporal indicators, such as the number of consecutive months in collection and the volume of occurrences in the last 3, 6, and 12 months. All temporal indicators are calculated dynamically based on the query's reference date, when provided.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Risk documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: collections.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.070
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.066
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.063
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.060
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.057
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 46,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT

0 Requests This Month

q

Family Online Presence
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns information related to the online presence of the respondent's family group, considering the presence and activity of family members on digital buying and selling platforms. The data allows for the analysis of digital behavior patterns, levels of digital inclusion, and the degree of presence of the family unit in online marketplaces.

The information is consolidated at the family level, supporting behavioral analyses, segmentations, and models that consider the group's digital context as a whole.

For more information on context, applications, and use cases related to digital behavior, please refer to the Behavior category documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: family_online_participation.

Price list

50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.118
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.112
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.106
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 86,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Election Service Providers
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset gathers information on contracts, service provision, and supplies related to election campaigns, involving the consulted entity and service providers or political candidates. The data includes details of each contract, such as the politician or party involved, contracted values, and information associated with the candidacy. For interpretation purposes, the data includes both services rendered and received by the entity, allowing for analysis of the direction of the contractual relationship within the electoral context.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Political Engagement documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: electoral_providers.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Indebtedness Question
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset indicates the probability of an individual being in default or having a negative credit rating, based on the analysis of public signals associated with their financial behavior. The return consists of a binary indicator, calculated by a statistical and machine learning model, that identifies patterns historically correlated with default.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Risk documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: indebtedness_question.

5.000.001 - 10.000.000	BRL 0.066
10.000.001 - 50.000.000	BRL 0.063
50,000,001 and above	BRL 0.060

Industrial Properties
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns information about trademarks and patents associated with the consulted CPF (Brazilian taxpayer ID), including distinct lists for each asset type and consolidated indicators such as total quantity, countries of registration, and average duration of extraordinary terms.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Economic and Financial documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: industrial_property.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.090
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.085
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.081
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.077
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.073
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 61,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
type	Define the type of asset being consulted (trademarks or patents).	By the way	For trademarks, add {brands} after the dataset. Ex: industrial_property{brands}. For patents, add {patents} after the dataset. Ex: industrial_property{patents}

To understand how to apply filters, sorting, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history

Limit

O pedido de patente será mantido em sigilo durante 18 (dezoito) meses a contar da data da prioridade mais antiga. Decorrido esse prazo, será publicado para conhecimento público. O depositante pode, porém, requerer a antecipação da publicação. O prazo de sigilo de 18 (dezoito) meses para o pedido de Certificado de Adição de Invencão é contado da data do depósito do pedido principal. Quando houver ocorrido a publicaçao do pedido principal, o pedido de Certificado de Adição de Invencão será imediatamente publicado. Os depósitos são designados de acordo com a natureza requerida: Invenção (PI), Modelo de Utilidade (MU) e Certificado de Adição de Invencão (C ). Family Financial Risk
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset consolidates financial, professional, and debt exposure indicators for the immediate family members of the person being surveyed, allowing for a broader view of financial risk within the family context. The analysis considers not only the individual but also information related to parents, spouse, and children, reflecting the impact of the family's financial situation on the consolidated risk score.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Risk documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: family_financial_risk.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.180
10.001 - 50.000	R$ 0.170
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.162
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.154
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.146
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	R$ 122,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Interpreting the fields FinancialRiskScore and FinancialRiskLevel

The field FinancialRiskScore This represents the individual's numerical financial risk score, ranging from 0 to 1000. The lower the score, the greater the identified financial risk. This score is calculated by weighting the indicators returned by the dataset according to predefined internal rules.

The field FinancialRiskLevel This translates the score into a categorical risk level, ranging from A (lowest risk) to H (highest risk), according to the intervals below.

FinancialRiskLevel	Score Value Range
THE	1000
B	670 - 999
W	500 - 669
D	420 - 499
AND	250 - 419
F	170 - 249
G	80 - 169
H	0 - 79
Ranges of returned values

Income estimates are presented in ranges of minimum wages (MW), while the wealth estimate (TotalAssetsThe value is presented in ranges in Brazilian Reais (R$). These ranges are used as input for calculating the score and the level of financial risk.

Field	Possible Values
EstimatedIncomeRange	NO INFORMATION, 0 TO 1 SM, 1 TO 2 SM, 2 TO 3 SM, 3 TO 5 SM, 5 TO 7 SM, 7 TO 10 SM, 10 TO 15 SM, 15 TO 20 SM, OVER 20 SM
TotalAssets	BELOW 100K, 100K TO 250K, 250K TO 500K, 500K TO 1MM, 1 TO 5MM, ABOVE 5MM

Financial Risk
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset consolidates economic, professional, and debt exposure indicators to estimate an individual's financial risk. By combining information such as estimated income, assets, professional affiliations, shareholdings, recent debt collection history, and receipt of government assistance, the dataset returns a score and a financial risk level, allowing for quick and standardized analyses.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Risk documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: financial_risk.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Interpreting the fields FinancialRiskScore and FinancialRiskLevel

The field FinancialRiskScore This represents the individual's numerical financial risk score, ranging from 0 to 1000. The lower the score, the greater the identified financial risk. This score is calculated by weighting the indicators returned by the dataset according to predefined internal rules.

The field FinancialRiskLevel This translates the score into a categorical risk level, ranging from A (lowest risk) to H (highest risk), according to the intervals below.

FinancialRiskLevel	Score Value Range
THE	1000
B	670 - 999
W	500 - 669
D	420 - 499
AND	250 - 419
F	170 - 249
G	80 - 169
H	0 - 79
Ranges of returned values

Income estimates are presented in ranges of minimum wages (MW), while the wealth estimate (TotalAssetsThe value is presented in ranges in Brazilian Reais (R$). These ranges are used as input for calculating the score and the level of financial risk.

Field	Possible Values
EstimatedIncomeRange	NO INFORMATION, 0 TO 1 SM, 1 TO 2 SM, 2 TO 3 SM, 3 TO 5 SM, 5 TO 7 SM, 7 TO 10 SM, 10 TO 15 SM, 15 TO 20 SM, OVER 20 SM
TotalAssets	BELOW 100K, 100K TO 250K, 250K TO 500K, 500K TO 1MM, 1 TO 5MM, ABOVE 5MM

Public Servants
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset gathers aggregated and detailed information about the individual's links to public administration, allowing for the identification of positions held, agencies of activity, and salary records over time. The result consolidates historical and current data on performance as a public servant, complementing the understanding of professional history in governmental contexts.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Professionals documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: profession_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
sector	Professional affiliation sector	Equal	PUBLIC
companyname	Name of the public body or entity	Contains	Any organ name
area	Job scope	Contains	Any area
level	Position or function performed	Contains	Any job description
status	Employment status	Equal	ACTIVE, INACTIVE

To understand how to apply filters, sorting, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Interpreting the field Status

The field Status This indicates the current status of the employment relationship as a public servant and may reflect the values. ACTIVE or INACTIVEThis status is defined based on an inference that considers explicit information from the sources, termination dates when available, and reference dates from payment records.

When the termination date is not provided, the last payment reference is used as the basis. If the individual remains without a payment record in public bodies for up to 6 months, the employment relationship is considered to have occurred. INACTIVEThis verification is applied only to the most recently identified public link for the entity; historical links are always considered inactive.

People

Companies
POST
Phone numbers of related people.
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns phone numbers associated with people related to the queried individual, with attributes for classification, validation, relationship, and prioritization. The results allow for the analysis of contacts linked to family members, partners, colleagues, or other types of relationships, supporting network analysis, segmentation, and automated decision-making.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Contacts documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: related_people_phones.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the accepted supplementary keys that allow you to refine the query or restrict the return to specific types of relationships between people.

Key	Description	Possible Values
relationshiptype	Restricts callbacks to phone numbers of people who have the specified type of relationship.	Types of relationships

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

Filters

The filters below allow you to refine the results according to phone characteristics and the type of relationship identified.

Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
relationshiptype	Type of relationship between the person being consulted and the phone number holder.	Equal	coworker, neighbor, brother, nephew, mother, son, household, grandson, spouse, relative, grandparent, uncle, cousin, father, partner, related
type	phone type	Equal	mobile, work, home
isactive	Indicates whether the phone is active.	Equal	true, false
isrecent	Indicates whether the phone is recent.	Equal	true, false
ismain	Indicates whether the phone is the primary phone.	Equal	true, false
isindonotcalllist	Indicates whether the phone number is on any block lists.	Equal	true, false
areacode	Telephone area code	Equal	11, 21, 61, ...

To understand how to apply filters, sorting, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month
BODY PARAMS
q
string
required

A string that defines the query's input parameters, used to specify the identifiers and filters applicable to the dataset.

Datasets
string
required

String containing the technical names of the datasets to be queried.

Limit
number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

RESPONSE
200

Dataset return parameters.

RESPONSE BODY
object
Result
array of objects
required

Result lists

object
RelatedPeoplePhones
array of objects
required
object
RelationshipType
string
required

Indicates the type of relationship associated with this record. (coworker, neighbor, brother, nephew, mother, son, household, grandson, spouse, relative, grandparent, uncle, cousin, father, partner, related)

Number
string
required

Phone number associated with the entity.

AreaCode
string
required

Phone number area code.

CountryCode
string
required

Phone number country code.

Complement
string
required

Complement (extension, etc...).

Type
string
required

Indicates the type of phone (WORK, MOBILE, HOME).

PhoneTotalPassages
integer
required

Number of times this phone number was seen for this entity.

PhoneBadPassages
integer
required

Number of times this phone number was seen for this entity in a suspicious situation.

PhoneCrawlingPassages
integer
required

Number of phone passes with the entity originating a capture.

PhoneValidationPassages
integer
required

Number of phone passes with the entity originating from a validation.

PhoneQueryPassages
integer
required

Number of telephone passes with the entity that originates a query.

PhoneMonthAveragePassages
integer
required

Average number of times the phone was seen with the entity per month.

PhoneNumberOfEntities
integer
required

Number of distinct entities related to the telephone.

Last3MonthsPassages
integer
required

Total number of passages for this phone in the last 3 months.

Last6MonthsPassages
integer
required

Total number of passages for this phone in the last 6 months.

Last12MonthsPassages
integer
required

Total number of passages for this phone in the last 12 months.

Last18MonthsPassages
integer
required

Total number of passages for this phone in the last 18 months.

Priority
integer
required

Priority level of this phone number for this entity.

IsMain
boolean
required

Flag indicating whether this is the entity's primary telephone number.

IsRecent
boolean
required

Flag indicating whether this phone number has been recently seen for this entity.

IsActive
boolean
required

Flag that indicates whether this phone number is active for this entity.

IsInDoNotCallList
boolean
required

Flag indicating whether this phone number is registered in a PROCON 'Do Not Call List'.

CurrentCarrier
string
required

Current carrier name of the phone number.

PlanType
string
required

Plan type (PREPAID, CONTROL, REGULAR, OTHER, UNKNOWN).

LastValidationDate
string
required

Date this phone number was last validated.

FirstPassageDate
string
required

Date this phone number was first seen for this entity.

LastPassageDate
string
required

Date this phone number was last seen for this entity.

CreationDate
string
required

Date the record was created.

LastUpdateDate
string
required

Date the record was last updated.

PortabilityHistory
array of objects
required

List of objects with information about the phone number portability history.

object
OriginalCarrier
string
required

Phone number original carrier name.

NewCarrier
string
required

Name of the new operator of the phone number.

PortabilityDate
string
required

Date the phone number was ported.

HasOptIn
boolean
required

Flag that indicates whether this phone number has been explicitly accepted in our database.

QueryId
string
required

Unique caller ID.

ElapsedMilliseconds
number
required

The number of milliseconds elapsed during the call.

QueryDate
string
required

The date when the request was received and processed.

Status
object
required

Status list for each dataset.

VIEW ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES
Evidences
object
required

List of evidence used for each dataset.

Phones
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns phone numbers associated with the queried person, with classification, validation, and prioritization attributes that support contact analysis, segmentation, data modeling, and automated decision-making. The return includes individual information for the identified phone numbers and consolidated aggregate indicators.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Contacts documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: phones_extended.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the accepted supplementary keys that allow you to refine the query or change how phone data is parsed and returned.

Key	Description	Possible Values
phone	Additional phone number used to enrich or refine the query, regardless of direct association with the document provided.	Phone number
returnonlydifferentphones	It only returns phone numbers that are different from those provided in the query.	true, false
withmatchrate	It calculates the similarity level between the entered phone number and the returned phone numbers.	true, false

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

Filters

The filters below allow you to refine the returned results according to specific characteristics of the identified phone numbers.

Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
type	phone type	Equal	mobile, work, home
isactive	Indicates whether the phone is active.	Equal	true, false
isrecent	Indicates whether the phone is recent.	Equal	true, false
ismain	Indicates whether the phone is the primary phone.	Equal	true, false
isindonotcalllist	Indicates whether the phone number is on any block lists.	Equal	true, false
areacode	Telephone area code	Equal	11, 21, 61, ...

To understand how to apply filters, sorting, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.

Professional Turnover
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset consolidates aggregated indicators on the professional turnover of the person surveyed, considering the frequency, duration, and variation of employment relationships over time. The feedback provides a summarized and analytical view of the history of professional changes, allowing for the evaluation of patterns of stability or high turnover without the need to individually analyze each identified employment relationship.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Professionals documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: professional_turnover.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Vehicles Associated with the Person
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas
Description

This dataset returns information about vehicles associated with the person being searched, including cars, motorcycles, trucks, and other types. The links between individuals and vehicles are mostly inferred from advertisements published on online buying and selling portals and do not necessarily imply ownership of the vehicle by the individual. In many cases, the records represent vehicles that the person has already sold, is advertising to third parties, or simply brokered the transaction for.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Vehicles documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: vehicles.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Filters
Field	Filter description	Filter type	Possible Filter Values
Model	vehicle model	Contains	Any keyword related to the model.
Category	Vehicle category	Equal	Values ​​defined in the category table.
FuelType	Type of fuel	Equal	GASOLINE, ALCOHOL, DIESEL, FLEX, etc.
Brand	vehicle make	Contains	Any brand keyword
FipeCode	Vehicle code in the FIPE table	Equal	Any valid FIPE code
ModelYear	Vehicle model year	Equal	1997, 2006, 2019, etc.
Limit

This dataset allows you to limit the number of records returned using the modifier .limit(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Interpreting the field Category

The field Category Identifies the type of vehicle associated with the person and assumes standardized values ​​as per the table below.

Category	Description
CARS	Passenger vehicles in general
TRUCKS	Trucks and cargo vehicles
MOTORCYCLES	Motorcycles

Make a request to see history.

BODY PARAMS
q
string
required

A string that defines the query's input parameters, used to specify the identifiers and filters applicable to the dataset.

Datasets
string
required

String containing the technical names of the datasets to be queried.

Limit
number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

RESPONSE
200

Dataset return parameters.

POST
First consultation

Now that you have your valid credentials and tokens, it's time to make your first call .

This page was created to help you make your first call to the BigDataCorp APIs in a simple and secure way. Here you will find practical examples of requests to different entities, showing how to use your JWT token, configure the necessary headers, and interpret the received responses.

The goal is for you to be able to quickly validate the integration with our Data Platform, following the standard call format adopted in all BigDataCorp APIs.



Using your JWT Token

All calls to BigDataCorp APIs require authentication via JWT tokensusing the headers AccessToken and TokenId.

cURL
     --header 'AccessToken: SEU-ACCESSTOKEN' \
     --header 'TokenId: SEU-TOKENID' \

These tokens can be generated directly in the BDC Center or via the Tokens API .



💬

More details about authentication and security are available in the Authentication and Security section.



⚙️ Practical example of a call

Below you will find a real-world example of a request, demonstrating the standard format used on the BigDataCorp Data Platform .



People API | Basic Registration Data
cURL
curl --request POST \
     --url https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/pessoas \
     --header 'AccessToken: SEU-ACCESSTOKEN' \
     --header 'TokenId: SEU-TOKENID' \
     --header 'accept: application/json' \
     --header 'content-type: application/json' \
     --data '
{
  "q": "doc{NUMERO-DO-CPF}",
  "Datasets": "basic_data"
}
'


✅
If the authentication is correct:

The response will provide JSON content with the basic registration data of the CPF (Brazilian individual taxpayer registration number) consulted.
Otherwise, check if:

The token is valid and has not expired;
You headers AccessToken and TokenId They are correctly informed;
THE endpoint is correct (.../pessoas);
THE dataset The information provided is correct (basic_data);
THE CPF The information was correctly provided, within the parameter. q, in the following format: doc{NUMERO-DO-CPF}.


💡
Reminder:

The authentication format is the same for all Data Platform APIs . Simply adjust the endpoint according to the desired entity . For example:

• .../pessoas → calls to datasets of People

• .../empresas → calls to datasets of Companies




Updated about 2 months ago

First steps

Take your first steps in integrating with the BigDataCorp Data Platform: we've gathered everything you need to securely make your first API call .

In this section, you'll find everything you need to start using BigDataCorp's APIs safely and efficiently:

Prerequisites: Know what is needed before starting: credentials, access to the BDC Center, and user permissions.
First consultation: learn how to generate your AccessToken and TokenId, add them correctly to the request headers, and see practical examples of real calls to test your integration.
Tips and best practices: learn about our recommendations for security, token rotation, and integration stability.

Explore the pages in the sidebar menu and, if you have any questions, speak to the team responsible for your support.




Updated 4 months ago

Status codes and descriptions
Prerequisites
Did this page help you?
Yes

CADE processes
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/processos
Description

This dataset returns information about the procedural progress of cases analyzed or already processed by the Administrative Council for Economic Defense (CADE), including process identification data, type, interested parties, records, protocols, and progress associated with decisions related to the defense of economic order.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, see the Other Processes documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: cade_processes_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT	

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month
BODY PARAMS
q
string
required

A string that defines the query's input parameters, used to specify the identifiers and filters applicable to the dataset.

Datasets
string
required

String containing the technical names of the datasets to be queried.

Limit
number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

RESPONSE
200

Dataset return parameters.

RESPONSE BODY
object
Result
array of objects
required

Result lists

object
CadeProcessesData
object
required

The main object returned by the dataset, containing consolidated information related to the queried entity.

CADEPROCESSESDATA OBJECT
QueryId
string
required

Unique caller ID.

ElapsedMilliseconds
number
required

The number of milliseconds elapsed during the call.

QueryDate
string
required

The date when the request was received and processed.

Status
object
required

Status list for each dataset.

VIEW ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES
Evidences
object
required

List of evidence used for each dataset.

VIEW ADDITIONAL PROPERTIES

Updated about 1 month ago

1

2
     --url https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/processos \
3
     --header 'AccessToken: ACCESSTOKEN' \
4
     --header 'TokenId: TOKENID' \
5
     --header 'accept: application/json' \
6
     --header 'content-type: application/json' \
7
     --data '
8
{
9
  "Datasets": "cade_processes_data",
10
  "q": "processnumber{NUMBER}",
11
  "Limit": 1
12
}
13
'

Technical Specifications
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/produtos
Description

A dataset that gathers technical and supplementary information associated with products, organized in a hierarchical structure that consolidates general item data and specific details by capture source. The return may include primary and alternative identifiers, as well as technical characteristics, physical specifications, nutritional information, active ingredients, editorial data, or other relevant attributes, depending on the product type and availability from the sources.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Basic Data documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: extra_info_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing you to refine the query or change how the data is analyzed and returned.

Key	Description	Possible Values
isbn	International Standard Book Number	Valid ISBN code
brand	Product brand	Text
category	Product category	Text
search terms	Keyword search terms	Text

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

Pagination

When more records are available, it is possible to paginate the results using the modifier. .next(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

RECENT REQUESTS
Log in to see full request history
TIME	STATUS	USER AGENT

Limit

https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/produtos
Description

This dataset gathers information about current and historical images associated with products, captured from multiple sources. The output is structured hierarchically, consolidating general product data, aggregated indicators of image availability by source, and historical details of captures made, including volume and recurrence over time.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Basic Data documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: images_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing you to refine the query or change how the data is analyzed and returned.

Key	Description	Possible Values
isbn	International Standard Book Number	Valid ISBN code
brand	Product brand	Text
category	Product category	Text
search terms	Keyword search terms	Text

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

Pagination

When more records are available, it is possible to paginate the results using the modifier. .next(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Notes and ratings
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/produtos
Description

This dataset gathers information about ratings and reviews associated with the product being consulted, based on consolidated reviews captured from different sources. The result presents aggregated indicators, such as volume of reviews, best and worst ratings, variations over time, and review content, in addition to consolidated and detailed metrics by source, allowing for historical and comparative analysis of the product's reputation.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Reputation documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: review_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing you to refine the query or change how the data is analyzed and returned.

Key	Description	Possible Values
isbn	International Standard Book Number	Valid ISBN code
brand	Product brand	Text
category	Product category	Text
search terms	Keyword search terms	Text

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

Pagination

When more records are available, it is possible to paginate the results using the modifier. .next(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Related Products
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/produtos
Description

This dataset gathers information about products related to the searched item, based on the identification of associations observed in different data sources. The return consolidates indicators by source, such as the total volume of related products and the number of items belonging to the same category, in addition to detailing the types of relationships identified, such as recommended products, products also viewed, or products also purchased.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, see the Relationships documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: related_product_data.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact
Relevant supplementary keys

The keys below represent some of the complementary keys accepted in this dataset, allowing you to refine the query or change how the data is analyzed and returned.

Key	Description	Possible Values
isbn	International Standard Book Number	Valid ISBN code
brand	Product brand	Text
category	Product category	Text
search terms	Keyword search terms	Text

To see all available keys and understand how to combine them, see the Query Parameters section.

Pagination

When more records are available, it is possible to paginate the results using the modifier. .next(x) in the field Datasets.

To understand how to apply filters, limit the number of results, paginate, and customize the data returned, see the Customize Returns section.

Prerequisites

Everything you need before you start using the BigDataCorp Data Platform: credentials, tokens, and access to the BDC Center.

Before you begin integrating with BigDataCorp's APIs, it's important to ensure that all the conditions below are met. This page outlines the essential steps to enable your integration environment, from initial access to token and endpoint validation.

Meeting these requirements ensures that your API calls are properly authenticated and that the environment is ready for secure and stable execution.



1️⃣ Be an active customer

You need to have an active account with BigDataCorp. If you are not yet a client, please contact us at comercial@bigdatacorp.com.br to register and gain access to the BDC Center .



2️⃣ Have an onboarding user

In BDC Center, create a specific service user for integrations. This user will be responsible for calls made via the API. More details about user and password management are available in the Authentication and Security section.

⚠️
Attention:

Use a valid corporate email address and restrict access to the technical team responsible for the integration.



3️⃣ Generate the JWT Token

With the integration user created, generate their JWT token ; it is necessary to authenticate all API calls. You can generate the token:

Directly at the BDC Center, or
Through the Token API, to automate generation and rotation.

More information about token generation and rotation is available in Authentication and Security → Token Rotation .



4️⃣ Confirm access to the entity endpoint.

Verify that you have access to the endpoint of the entity you wish to integrate. All entities in the Data Platform use the same base domain:

https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/

Only the path changes depending on the entity. Here are some examples:

.../pessoas → calls to the People datasets
.../empresas → calls to the Companies datasets
.../ondemand → calls to on-demand datasets


5️⃣ Validate the integration environment

Before executing calls in production:

Test your requests with the generated token.
Validate the response and return format (examples available in First Query ).
Store tokens in environment variables, never in source code.


⚠️
Important:

The absence of any of the above items will prevent API calls from being executed. Be sure to complete all steps before proceeding to First Query .




Updated 2 months ago

Additional resources

Additional features to make your consultations more secure, traceable, and flexible.

This page presents advanced features that complement the use of queries on the BigDataCorp Data Platform.

Here you will learn how to:

Using hashes as an alternative to sending documents directly
Add tags to calls for tracking and organizing flows.
Explore metadata for further analysis of events and integrations.

These features help adapt queries to different security, privacy, and governance requirements.

1️⃣ Hashes as a lookup key

In some scenarios, the client may not feel comfortable directly sending their CPF (Brazilian individual taxpayer registration number) or CNPJ (Brazilian company taxpayer registration number) in a request, whether due to privacy concerns, internal rules, or operational simplicity .

To address these cases, the Data Platform allows the submission of hasheaded document as a lookup key, using the parameter dochashThe hash It must be generated in the standard format. SHA-256, widely adopted in the market.




Example:

JSON
{
    "Datasets": "addresses",
    "q": "dochash{A457CEBA5B8481AFF573336A02A1BB022BD43056F48B022AD0C6320E87EF9EF9}"
}



This feature allows you to perform queries while maintaining compatibility with the APIs, without directly exposing the original document.

2️⃣ Call tags

Tags are custom identifiers used to categorize, organize, and add context to calls made on the Data Platform.

They function as metadata in the format chave: valor, being recorded along with the request information.

All of the Platform's APIs allow the sending of tags.

Among the main uses of tags are:

Identifying workflows (e.g., onboarding, credit analysis)
Differentiation of environments (e.g., testing, production)
Traceability of integrations and internal processes.
Further analysis via the Events API

Examples:

Simple use of tags
JSON
{
  "Datasets": "basic_data",
  "q": "doc{xxxxxxxxxxx}",
  "Tags": {
    "my_key": "my_value",
    "environment": "production"
  }
}



Tags applied to a specific flow
JSON
{
  "Datasets": "registration_data,kyc,occupation_data",
  "q": "doc{xxxxxxxxxxx}",
  "Tags": {
    "host": "HOST_ID",
    "process": "ONBOARDING"
  }
}



📘

These tags can be used as filters in the Events API, allowing you to retrieve calls related to a specific process or business flow.



Rules for using tags

For the request to be processed correctly, the tags must follow the rules below:

You are allowed to send a maximum of 10 tags per request.
Keys and values ​​must be between 1 and 255 characters long .
To the keys can contain only letters of A to Z (without accents) or the character _ (underline).
Values ​​can contain letters, numbers, spaces, and the following special characters: _ * - + , . : ! @ # & /


✨ From here, you've mastered the essential features and are ready to move forward in using the specific APIs and datasets of the Data Platform.



Updated 2 months ago

Customize return

Welcome

Begin your integration journey with BigDataCorp. Here, you'll find instructions, examples, and best practices for connecting to our products and APIs.

👋 Welcome to the BigDataCorp technical documentation!

Here you'll find everything you need to get started — and grow — with BigDataCorp products. Our goal is to provide clear instructions, usage examples, and best practices to ensure secure, scalable, and efficient integrations.

In this documentation, you will be able to:

To become familiar with the datasets on the BigDataCorp Data Platform .
Understanding how API calls, parameters, and responses work.
Consult detailed instructions regarding authentication and security, as well as status codes .
Follow the integration examples available in Getting Started .
Explore the endpoints of each entity and their respective datasets.


🔎 How to navigate

The documentation has been organized to make navigation simple and intuitive. Use the side menu to quickly find what you need:

Overview: Presents the main concepts and guidelines on Authentication and Security and Status Codes .
First Steps: provides a quick and practical guide to get you started with your integration, covering prerequisites, token generation, and your first API call.
API navigation: explore the specific endpoints for each entity (person, company, product, etc.), organized by dataset sets, with usage examples and detailed parameters.


Happy exploring! 🚀

Take the opportunity to explore our datasets and discover how BigDataCorp's data can boost your solutions.

Historical Vehicle License Plate Data
POST
https://plataforma.bigdatacorp.com.br/vehicles
Description

This dataset returns registration and historical information associated with a vehicle license plate, including data such as make, model, year, color, registered alerts, and the history of the plate's links to different vehicles over time. The query uses a historical database with data up to July 2021; Mercosur standard license plates can be entered, and will be automatically converted to the old model for query purposes. Newer vehicles, registered after this period, may not have available information.

For more information on business context, applications, and use cases, please refer to the Basic Data documentation.

Technical name of the dataset: license_plates.

Price list
Queries Held in the Month	Value per query
1 - 10.000	BRL 0.050
10.001 - 50.000	BRL 0.048
50.001 - 100.000	BRL 0.046
100.001 - 500.000	BRL 0.044
500.001 - 1.000.000	BRL 0.042
1.000.001 - 5.000.000	BRL 34,000.00 (fixed price)
5,000,001 and above	Contact

Log in to see full request history

Make a request to see history.
0 Requests This Month
BODY PARAMS
q
string
required

A string that defines the query's input parameters, used to specify the identifiers and filters applicable to the dataset.

Datasets
string
required

String containing the technical names of the datasets to be queried.

Limit
number

Maximum number of entities returned in the response, used in queries performed without a primary key. Maximum allowed value: 80

RESPONSE
200

Dataset return parameters.

RESPONSE BODY
object
Result
array of objects
required

object

object
required

The main object returned by the dataset, containing consolidated information related to the queried entity.

LICENSEPLATEDATA OBJECT

string
required

number
required

The number of milliseconds elapsed during the call.
